plugins.name: todo
plugins.description: 0.1.0 (2026-06-07) /todo — cross-domain TODO + multi-track launcher. Reads the repo's `DOMAINS.tape` roster + each domain snapshot, ranks domains by OPEN milestone count (`- [ ]`), and renders a numbered dashboard (★ active · 5-cell progress bar · open count · @goal snippet, sorted open-desc). Select a subset to drive in parallel: `/todo <n> <m> …` picks by number; `/todo <N> track` auto-picks the top-N most-open domains as N tracks (e.g. `/todo 2 track` → the two domains with the most open work). The pick PERSISTS a track set under `~/.sidecar/todo/<repo-key>.tracks` and prints a per-track launch plan (`track i: <NAME> → /domain set <NAME> && /cycle-bg`); the MODEL then fans out ONE background Agent per track — each runs `/domain set <NAME>` then autonomously drains that domain's open milestones (isolation worktree · one `/pr-cycle` per logical unit), the same command-prints-plan → model-fans-out contract as `/cycle`. Verbs — bare/`ls`/`list` (dashboard) · `<n…>` or `<N> track` (select + plan) · `go`/`plan` (re-print the saved plan) · `status` (show track set) · `clear` (drop it). Pairs with the `domain` plugin (roster + snapshots are its SSOT) but is strictly READ-ONLY over the repo — it never edits a domain doc / verdict / log; the only state written is the track set. Implemented in bash (`bin/_todo.sh`); the `/domain todo <task>` log verb is unrelated (this is a separate launcher plugin).

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: 0.25.1 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.25.0 (2026-06-02) adds @D g79 (NN/training stack — flame + forge only, never PyTorch/ATen: all neural-net/training/tensor-autograd work routes through hexa-native flame [stdlib/flame compiler-only NN] on forge [self/forge GPU substrate]; direct PyTorch/ATen forbidden in ANY repo), in lockstep with its enforcement — the `flame-forge-guard` 0.1.0 PreToolUse(Bash·Write|Edit) global deny hook (s7; flame:forge :: torch:ATen); commons @V 2.2→2.3. 0.24.0 (2026-06-01) commons.tape @V 2.1→2.2 + g71 rewritten — pool-route auto-dispatch removed (pool-route 0.21.0), so g71 drops the stale 'local sign-gate' phrasing (sign local removed) and states pool work as explicit-dispatch + sync-to-origin/main only. 0.23.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g0..g77` form, no tool/usage) + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json) + a live cwd `./pods.json` per-project pod/job manifest snapshot appended per injection so the host lineup AND the /system SSOT are visible without running `pool list` / `hexa cloud pods` first; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades; SessionStart additionally appends COMMANDS.md (the cross-project slash-command catalog) once per session. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT). 0.23.0 (2026-06-01) splits commons.tape into HARD/SOFT tiers — the per-turn UserPromptSubmit injects HARD rules only (`_filter_hard()` drops `@D` blocks whose header bracket carries the ` soft` marker), session-boundary events (SessionStart · PreCompact · PostCompact) inject the FULL set; safety/routing/ship/honesty rules stay sharp every turn while style + niche-command-pointer rules (g3 · g14 · g40 · g42 · g43 · g44 · g51 · g53 · g56 · g70) surface at boundaries without burying the hard layer (per-turn payload −~7KB). Default is hard; add ` soft` to a rule's bracket to demote it. 0.22.0 (2026-05-30) extends @D g78 with the canonical code-tier mount root `~/mnt/<name>` ($HOME-relative · portable s3 · registered via `sidecar mount add <name> <host>:<repo>`) — pins the sshfs mountpoint convention into governance so every session knows the code-tier mount lives at `~/mnt/` (live: `mini:core/hexa-lang` → `~/mnt/mini-hexa`); commons @V 2.0→2.1. 0.21.0 (2026-05-30) added @D g78 (code-tier — edit a remote repo over sshfs, build on its LOCAL fs · generic `sidecar mount` launcher); commons @V 1.9→2.0. 0.20.0 (2026-05-30) sharpens @D g47 (PR ship discipline) with a learned operational rule — run `gh pr merge` / `--delete-branch` from the CANONICAL repo dir, NOT a linked worktree's cwd (a worktree holds 'main', so merging from inside it errors `main is already used by worktree`); commons @V 1.8→1.9. The git-guard `git worktree remove --force` false-positive is filed as a separate source-fix handoff (g11/g59), not worked around. 0.19.0 (2026-05-30) adds @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login — run `secret list` + check known aliases huggingface.token↔hf.token before claiming a credential missing; the 2026-05-30 HF-token incident), shipped in lockstep with its enforcement — the new `secret-guard` 0.1.0 PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook (s7 — rule + enforcement together). 0.18.0 (2026-05-29) renders M5 pod project attribution in `_pods_snapshot()` — each rented pod shows `<id>[<provider>,<status>] @<project> · "<purpose>"` (empty/absent project → `@⚠unattributed`, purpose omitted when empty), so a SessionStart reader sees WHICH project owns each billing pod (matches the hexa-lang `pod_registry.hexa` project/purpose fields + the cross-project `cloud down` guard — anima RTSC near-miss fix). 0.16.0 (2026-05-29) sharpens @D g8 into an explicit deny+redirect — ALL direct runpod/vast provider CLI (any verb incl. read/lifecycle) + raw ssh/scp/curl + REST/GraphQL API + python/node SDK forbidden, all access routes through `hexa cloud` full pod lifecycle (rent·down·list·exec·run·nohup·copy·preflight); commons @V 1.5→1.6 · lockstep with cloud-guard 0.3.0 enforcement (s7). 0.13.0 (2026-05-29 · CLOUD M10) repoints `_pods_snapshot()` to the GLOBAL M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` (cwd-independent · cost-bearing · `<id>[<provider>,<status>]` + `$<cost>`) + adds `_providers_snapshot()` (M8 catalog `~/.hx/cloud/providers.json` one-liner). Both global → always visible · absent → silent. 0.12.0 (2026-05-28) added `_pods_snapshot()` on cwd `./pods.json` (superseded by M5 global registry); pair feature with `pods-route` hook (auto `--register`). 0.11.0 (2026-05-28) drops the `enabled` per-host field + `[os,on|off]` rendering from the roster snapshot — roster hosts are always-on (pool 0.8.9 ships the matching always-on model on the CLI side); the SessionStart roster line now renders `name[os]` and pi5-akida exclusion is handled downstream by pool-route's name-substring akida match. 0.10.15 adds @D g75 (candidate orchestration — build phase vs fire phase split, domain-agnostic — pairs with micro-exp 0.4.0 Stage 1.5 + cycle 0.9.2 decision tree).

plugins.name: code-tier-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block enforcing commons @D g78 (code-tier — edit a remote repo over its sshfs mount, never a local clone), implemented in hexa-lang (`_code_tier_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads ~/.sidecar/codetier.conf (`name|host:remote_dir|mountpoint|localpath`); for any tier that declares a 4th `localpath`, a Write/Edit/NotebookEdit or Bash write channel (`>`, `>>`, tee, dd of=, cp/mv dest; relative resolved against payload cwd) whose target resolves UNDER that local clone is denied with an action-first redirect to the same relative path under the tier's MOUNT. SAFETY RAILS (skip-on-doubt): `.git/` under the local clone is exempt (local git stays usable) · the mountpoint itself + anything under it is exempt (correct edit path) · a target not under any registered localpath passes · a tier whose localpath equals its mountpoint is ignored · absent config or no localpath fields → inert (allows everything). Gate is a real precondition (registered localpath + target under it), not an opt-out switch (project.tape @D s11); enforcement is the hard deny (s13). Pairs with the `sidecar mount` launcher (registers tiers + the localpath field). 0.1.0 (2026-05-30) initial — lands in lockstep with commons g78 + `sidecar mount` (s7: rule + enforcement together). NO env-var / flag override by design.

plugins.name: engine-map-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) GROWS TEETH (guard-teeth-sweep @L2 · s7) — no longer advisory-only; now carries a SIGN-GATED SEVERITY DIAL read from the enclosing anima project.tape `@C engine-map` block (`mode = "force" | "warn" | "off"`, hexa-native precedent). force (DEFAULT — also the fail-safe when the dial is absent / unknown / unreadable) = HARD-DENY (emits permissionDecision:"deny", the write is blocked) · warn = ALLOW + the reason rides in permissionDecisionReason (the prior advisory behavior, de-duped once per session/file) · off = silent pass. The dial lives in the sign-gated SSOT (project.tape @D s13), so LOWERING severity needs a fresh user `sidecar sign project` — it can NOT auto-flip to silence, which is why it is NOT the env-var/config opt-out @D s11 forbids (s11 carries the explicit sanctioned EXCEPTION; cf hexa-native @C hexa-native). Re-added to the marketplace roster (the 0.1.0 entry was accidentally dropped during the hf-recover consolidation b270511). Both invariant detectors UNCHANGED. PreToolUse(Edit|Write), implemented in hexa-lang (`_engine_map_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reconstructs the proposed post-edit content from the PreToolUse payload (Write content · Edit old_string/new_string/replace_all). Invariant 1 (a_lane_akida_gpu_split, on a `.md` / `.log.md` doc edit): a single result/verdict line that CONFLATES a Lane-A substrate term (AKIDA / AKD1000 / pi5-akida / on-chip-non-det) with a Lane-G/GPU term (GPU / H100 / forge / cuBLAS) AND carries a verdict/number token (🟢/🔴/CE/util/PASS/RED/=) WITHOUT an explicit AKIDA+GPU two-tag separation (a lane separator |/;/ vs /→). Invariant 2 (a_core_engine_map, on a `CORE/*.hexa` edit of pure_field.hexa / engine_g.hexa / brain.hexa): a direct import/read of `.clm` / `.kosmos` / `kosmos_io` in those 3 protected engine files (bypassing the CORE/generator.hexa L3 slot + kosmos_io→brain_decide read). Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. 0.1.0 (2026-06-02) initial advisory version.

plugins.name: prefs
plugins.description: /prefs — view or set language preferences (3 axes: code authoring · doc authoring · response language to user). /prefs show · /prefs code <lang> · /prefs docs <lang> · /prefs response <lang>. Writes the FIXED SSOT ~/.claude/plugins/data/prefs-sidecar/prefs.json (defaults code=english · docs=english · response=korean) via `_prefs.hexa` (shipped in `prefs-hook`). The `prefs-hook` plugin reads the same SSOT and auto-injects current values every turn. Command half of the prefs split.

plugins.name: prefs-hook
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects the user's current language preferences (code · docs · response axes) as additionalContext from the FIXED SSOT ~/.claude/plugins/data/prefs-sidecar/prefs.json (written by the `prefs` command plugin); re-injects every turn so the preference never fades from context. Type-gated on read (a non-string field falls back to the default). hexa-lang (`_prefs.hexa inject`, via `hexa run`). Hook half of the prefs split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: recommend-axes
plugins.description: 0.5.0 (2026-06-06) NEW `resolve-mode <args...>` verb — a DETERMINISTIC /sbs·/step-by-step mode resolver (s7 teeth for recommend-default inheritance): reads `~/.sidecar/recommend-default` authoritatively and computes the LOCKED precedence IN CODE — EXPLICIT `auto:<axis>`/`auto:<weights>`/`manual`/`legacy-manual` > inherited FIXED axis (bare `auto` / no token) > 4-axis 1:1:1:1 > MANUAL — printing human `mode: <…>[ ← inherited from recommend-default]` + machine `resolved: mode=.. axis=.. weights=.. source=explicit|inherited|default`. sbs.md/step-by-step.md Step 1 `!`-exec it and OBEY (authoritative); the in-context `# default mode:` directive stays the graceful fallback. Replaces 100%-model-prose mode reading with code-computed precedence. Existing verbs (inject·show·set-default·clear-default·get-default) untouched. 0.4.1 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.3.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — injects the 4-axis recommendation rubric (완성도 complete · 단순 simple · 안전 safe · 표준 std) as additionalContext from the SSOT carrier `recommend.tape` shipped in the plugin dir; re-injects every turn so the rubric never fades. The rule: whenever the model makes ANY recommendation, it presents FOUR parallel champion options — one per axis (① 완성도 robustness/edge-cases · ② 단순 Occam/fewest-parts · ③ 안전 minimal blast radius/reversible · ④ 표준 fits the established pattern), each with a one-line rationale, NOT a single weighted-sum winner; when all four axes converge it collapses to one '전축 합의: <안>' line. 0.2.0 adds an OPTIONAL DEFAULT MODE (r4) stored in `$HOME/.sidecar/recommend-default` (one token), with THREE modes: PRESENT (= absent, the original 4축제시 — render the box, user picks each time) · AUTO (4축 합의기준 자동 — score candidates on all four axes, weighted avg 1:1:1:1, tie→안전, auto-pick the consensus winner + render a `🤖 4축 auto-pick: <안> (…weighted=<sum>)` conclusion line under the box) · FIXED-AXIS (complete|simple|safe|std — 임의 고정 선택 · auto-proceed). AUTO + FIXED both STILL render all four lines (a LEAN/conclusion, never a collapse; NOT an s11 opt-out). Set/clear/read via bin verbs `set-default <mode>` · `clear-default` (→ present) · `get-default`. Generalizes the 4-axis lens `/sbs auto` uses for disambiguation to EVERY recommendation surfaced. 0.3.0 makes FIXED-AXIS an AUTO-PROCEED mode — it was ★-mark-and-wait, which never advanced (user set `complete`, model still stopped at the box); now a fixed axis renders the box (★ on the fixed axis) THEN auto-proceeds with that axis's champion as the decision (decide for the user, do NOT wait — like AUTO but the winning axis is pre-fixed, not scored) + a `🤖 고정축 auto-pick: <안> (<axis> 기준)` conclusion line. hexa-lang (`_recommend.hexa inject`, via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design. 0.4.0 — the injected `# default mode:` directive is now the SINGLE SSOT for /sbs·/step-by-step mode too: both the AUTO and the FIXED <axis> directive strings carry one added clause that they ALSO govern /sbs mode resolution when no explicit mode token is given (FIXED <axis> → sbs runs AUTO with that single axis forced · auto-proceed · agreement screen still once; AUTO → sbs runs AUTO 4-axis 1:1:1:1; directive absent/PRESENT → sbs MANUAL). This removes sbs's skippable `~/.sidecar/recommend-default` file-read step (the s7 anti-pattern that left bare `/sbs <task>` silently falling back to MANUAL); sbs now OBEYS the in-context directive instead of re-reading the file.

plugins.name: hexa-lsp

Spawns via `sh -c` that cd's into the first candidate dir actually holding `self/lsp.hexa` — `$HEXA_LANG` → `$HOME/.hx/packages/hexa` → `$HOME/.hx/packages/hexa-lang` (legacy) → `$HOME/.hx/src` — before `exec hexa lsp` (upstream resolves `self/lsp.hexa` relative to cwd; the file-presence guard avoids the silent `cd .` fallback that surfaced as the /doctor 'source file not found' exit-1 crash trail). Minimal: only `.hexa` claimed.

plugins.name: project-tape
plugins.description: 0.2.2 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreCompact + PostCompact hook — re-injects <project-root>/project.tape as additionalContext so the project's identity + governance survive auto-compaction. No-op when project.tape is absent.

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: 0.8.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) + SessionStart git-staleness safety guard, in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa` + `_git_guard_session.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Five layers, all no-network (FETCH_HEAD mtime is the freshness proxy — the hook NEVER runs `git fetch`). DOWNSTREAM: (1) DENIES force-type push — `git push --force` / `-f` · `--force-with-lease` · `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force); quotes stripped before tokenizing (0.4.2) so `'--force-with-lease=x'` / `+"refspec"` can't slip past. (2) STALE-BASE PUSH advisory (0.5.0, non-blocking): for a non-force push, emits additionalContext when the current branch trails the remote default base (origin/HEAD, else origin/main) by >= 20 commits — the shared-workdir branch-swap / silent-revert fingerprint; push still proceeds. (3) STALE-BASE MERGE advisory (0.6.0, anima #1105, non-blocking): a `gh pr merge` (squash) reaches main via a path the push-time check never saw — it revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 — so when the command contains `gh pr merge` (no `git push`, not cross-repo `--repo X`), runs the same divergence probe and warns before the merge lands (real deny on mass deletion lives in pr-cycle-hook's deletion-sanity gate). UPSTREAM (0.7.0): (4) CREATION advisory — on `git worktree add` / `git checkout -b` / `git switch -c`, warns when the base does not resolve to `origin/<default>` (local main/HEAD/omitted) OR `.git/FETCH_HEAD` is older than 30 min; steers to `git fetch origin` then base off `origin/<default>`. (5) SessionStart PROACTIVE ff-only auto-sync (0.8.0, was advisory) — `safe_ff_sync()` SILENTLY fast-forwards a clean tree strictly behind origin/<default> (`git fetch && git merge --ff-only`); motivation 2026-05-30 — a 28-commit-behind local repo nearly landed a STALE project.tape that would have REVERTED origin governance (the old warn-only layer was exactly what got ignored). Happy path emits NOTHING. SAFETY RAILS: ff-only (NEVER merge-commit/rebase/force) · clean-tree-only · skip-on-doubt (no origin · detached · mid-rebase/merge · diverged) · FETCH_HEAD-gated fetch (>5min). Surfaces EXACTLY ONE line only on a true non-ff divergence; NEVER denies. Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: workdir-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. SessionStart advisory (hexa-lang `_workdir_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`) that fires once per session, and only when the current git working tree is shared by >= 2 concurrent `claude` agents. Detection (~0.1s): `ps` lists claude PIDs (comm == 'claude' — lsof's command field shows the version string, not 'claude'), then one `lsof -a -p <list> -d cwd` collects their cwds, awk prefix-matched against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. A shared working tree = shared `.git/index` + `HEAD`, so a sibling agent can swap HEAD out from under you, commingle your staged files, or leave your push on a stale branch. Injects a one-shot reminder to do git landings in an isolated worktree and never reset/switch/delete another agent's active branch. Grouped by working tree, not repo — silent in solo sessions (count 1) and self-extinguishes once you move into a worktree. Advisory-only, non-blocking. Proactive complement to git-guard's reactive stale-base push advisory. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: worktree-guard
plugins.description:
0.2.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) advisory for `git worktree add`, in hexa-lang (`_worktree_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When a Bash command creates a git worktree, emits non-blocking systemMessage with the durable-worktree drill: in a heavily-concurrent or synced repo an isolated worktree dir can be removed out from under you (a sibling agent's `git worktree prune`, a workspace sync, or the macOS /tmp reaper) and any UNCOMMITTED edits vanish with it — so commit + push promptly after editing, don't hold uncommitted work across turns, land via PR then `git worktree remove` (verification builds AFTER the commit, so a failed build never costs the work). Stronger note when the target path is under a volatile location (/tmp · /private/tmp · /var/folders). BRANCH-REUSE advisory (0.2.0, anima #1105): when `git worktree add -b
` reuses an existing branch — especially a LOCAL-only stale one absent from origin (a rejected/unpushed prior turn's leftover) — warns of the stale-base risk that revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 and suggests a fresh per-PR branch (`
-$(date +%s)` from origin/main). Advisory-only (guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking systemMessage > deny for hygiene). Sister of workdir-guard (shared-tree SessionStart advisory) + worktree-gc (merged-worktree prune). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: plan-guard
plugins.description: 0.4.0 (2026-06-06) @L SHIP-GATE — gives the `@L` locked-decision lint TEETH at ship time (s7 gap: the engine `_plan_lint.hexa` exits nonzero on an unmet assert, but every plan-guard hook only emitted advisory additionalContext, so the 'enforce plan adherence' promise had no teeth). New PreToolUse(Bash) gate `_plan_ship_gate.hexa` fires ONLY on a SHIP command (`git commit` / `gh pr create` / `gh pr merge`), resolves the ACTIVE plan (same resolver as `_plan_inject` / `_plan_ship`: frontmatter `status: active`, else newest non-closed `drafts/*-plan.md`), runs the PR-2 plan-lint engine (single source of truth) against the staged/committed diff, and when ANY `@L` assert is UNMET consults a SIGN-GATED SEVERITY DIAL. SAFETY — DEFAULT = warn, NOT force: blocking a commit is high blast-radius, so this gate is the DEFAULT-SAFE sibling of hexa-native / engine-map (which fail-safe to `force`); THIS gate fails-safe to `warn` — absent / unknown / unreadable `project.tape` → warn, so NO path blocks a commit by default. Dial = `@C plan-ship-gate` block in the repo's `project.tape` (`mode = "force" | "warn" | "off"`): force = permissionDecision "deny" (blocks the ship, unmet @L list as the reason) · warn (DEFAULT) = ALLOW + a stderr warning listing the unmet @L ids · off = silent pass-through. The dial lives in the sign-gated SSOT (project.tape @D s13) so LOWERING/RAISING it needs a fresh user `sidecar sign project` — it can NOT auto-flip to silence, the sanctioned s11 EXCEPTION (cf @C hexa-native), NOT an env-var/flag opt-out (none exists, no MODE=off escape). Real-precondition silent-allow gates only: non-ship command · no active plan · plan with no `@L` asserts · engine/hexa binary absent · all asserts met. Always exits 0 except the force-deny path (permissionDecision JSON on stdout, same wrapper as engine-map-guard). Existing _plan_inject / _plan_ship / _plan_lint behavior unchanged. PRIOR 0.3.4 (2026-06-06) PARSER FIX (guard-teeth-sweep @L9) — `_plan_lint.hexa` `_parse_l_line` now strips a surrounding matched quote pair from the assert `arg` (new `_strip_quotes` helper, applied right after the negate `!` strip): `assert:grep "quoted phrase"` was being grep'd WITH the literal double-quotes, a false-negative that failed even when the phrase was present. Leading+trailing `"` (both) OR `'` (both) removed; mismatched / one-sided / unquoted args unchanged. 0.3.3 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. Enforces step-by-step plan locked-decision adherence — the marker + hook + linter triad closing the three observed plan-drift root causes (orchestrator writes an escape into the sub-agent prompt that contradicts a locked decision · mid-design drift off the original plan · sbs Auto-QA conformance is a soft self-check that misses it). CORE (0.1.0): the `plan-lint` engine (`_plan_lint.hexa`, via `hexa run`) parses a plan.md's `## locked decisions` `@L<n> … assert:<kind> <arg>` machine-readable contract (step-by-step 0.8.0 marker) and verifies each assert against the repo or, with `--diff <files…>`, a restricted file set — `grep <pat>` (term present · `!pat`=absent) · `file <path>` (exists) · `verdict <slug>/<id>` (`.verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt` non-empty). Per-`@L` ✓/✗ verdict + NONZERO exit on any unmet; a plan with no `@L` lines is skipped (exit 0, backward-compatible). Advisory by design — the bundled hooks only emit additionalContext, never deny. Mirrors the memory-lint hexa-lint pattern (defensive readers · JSON-escaped additionalContext · no network). 0.2.0 adds the PreToolUse(Task|Agent) inject hook (`_plan_inject.hexa`): on every sub-agent spawn it resolves the ACTIVE plan (frontmatter `status: active`, else newest-mtime drafts/*-plan.md), extracts the `@L` locked-decision lines and injects them VERBATIM as additionalContext so the orchestrator can't silently write a contradicting escape into the spawn prompt (root cause #1 — the '.kosmos 필수' → 'SKIP OK' incident), plus an escape scan (skip/ignore/생략/… near a locked term or human-label token) that appends an advisory warning on a possible contradiction. Always exits 0 (advisory). 0.3.0 adds the PostToolUse(Bash) ship-lint hook (`_plan_ship.hexa`): fires after a ship command (`git commit` / `gh pr …`), runs the PR-2 plan-lint engine against the shipped diff (`git diff origin/main...HEAD` file set, else staged/HEAD, else repo-wide) and surfaces any UNMET `@L` asserts as a verbatim additionalContext warning so a mid-design drift off the locked plan (root cause #2, e.g. 'AKIDA-first → GPU') is caught at ship where sbs Auto-QA's soft conformance self-check (root cause #3) missed it. Non-blocking — always exits 0. 0.3.1 hardens active-plan resolution (@L6): the newest-mtime fallback now SKIPS plans explicitly closed (`status: done`/`abandoned`), so a flipped-done plan's `@L` is never enforced against a later unrelated session — paired with the step-by-step 0.8.1 closure flip. 0.3.2 documents the dogfood self-test (build plan's @L1~@L6 all PASS against landed artifacts; 2 honest findings logged).

plugins.name: subagent-resilience
plugins.description: 0.5.0 (2026-06-05) ENFORCE POLL-INLINE — adds a SubagentStop hook (`_resilience_stop_guard.hexa`) giving clause 6 TEETH. The 0.4.0 POLL-INLINE clause was ADVISORY-ONLY (PreToolUse:Agent inject, no enforcement); a real transcript showed a sub-agent launch a long background compile then repeatedly END ITS TURN ("Still compiling. Let me wait for the monitor event." / "Waiting for the monitor notification."), each end-turn firing a spurious "completed" notification to the parent while the agent never progressed. The new SubagentStop hook BLOCKS that stop (exit 2 + stderr steer → POLL INLINE) but ONLY when BOTH (a) PARKING-LANGUAGE in the last assistant message (case-insensitive: monitor event/notification · wait(ing)/hold for the monitor · let-me-wait+monitor) AND (b) a still-PENDING bg job (primary: payload's non-empty `background_tasks`; fallback: transcript tail shows a backgrounded Bash with no DONE marker). FALSE-POSITIVE GUARD (critical): a genuinely-finished agent SHORT-CIRCUITS to allow (PR url · pull-request opened/merged · ✅🟢🔵 · all-done/task-complete/final-report), and EITHER signal missing → ALLOW (exit 0); fail-open on any parse error. Wired via the same `sh -c … hexa run … rc==2→exit 2` wrapper so a hexa-missing env degrades to exit 0 (never traps an agent). Prior 0.4.0 (2026-06-05) POLL-INLINE — adds a 6th contract clause from a live lesson where, across ONE campaign, FOUR babysitter agents died the same way: each launched a long detached GPU job (a nohup train / remote run), then armed a Monitor/waiter to wait for it — but in this env arming a Monitor ENDS the agent turn immediately, orphaning the live job with no babysitter (it kept burning B200/H100 cost with nobody to harvest / recover / tear it down). Clause 6 POLL-INLINE tells the sub-agent: to wait on a long detached job, do NOT arm a Monitor and end your turn — BLOCK INLINE instead with one long-timeout Bash loop (`for i in $(seq 1 N); do <alive+progress check>; sleep 90; done`), re-issue until DONE, THEN harvest+recover+ship in the same turn. This is the sub-agent form of the a_cpu_local_no_waiter rule. Clauses 1-5 unchanged. Prior 0.3.0 (2026-06-04) STORM-HARDENED — strengthens clauses 1 (BACKOFF) + 2 (CHECKPOINT) from a live lesson where dispatched agents died at ~183 tokens in a SERVER-WIDE rate-limit storm with NOTHING pushed: BACKOFF now names the server-wide-storm-vs-usage-cap distinction + a longer schedule (30s->60s->120s->240s->480s, ~8 tries / ~15 min) so the first throttle hit isn't treated as fatal; CHECKPOINT now mandates a tiny WIP/skeleton commit IMMEDIATELY at START (before the real work) so even a first-call death leaves a resumable branch (the 0-progress-death root cause). Clauses 3 RESUME / 4 REPORT-ON-HALT / 5 STEER unchanged. Prior 0.2.0 (2026-06-01) adds a 5th contract clause — STEER: closes the cross-repo discoverability gap (handoff 623a9758, hexa-lang) where an agent hit the Agent-tool doc's `SendMessage` pointer (absent in this env) and TaskStop+relaunched to amend a sub-agent's scope. The contract now tells both parent and sub-agent that SendMessage does NOT exist here and the supported mid-run steer mechanism is the sidecar `walkie` skill (sub runs `/walkie on`, parent `/walkie call <handle> "<new scope>"`, `/walkie arm <handle>` prints the listen line to embed) — injected at the exact spawn-time choke point so the next dispatch can't miss it. 🪂 PreToolUse(Task|Agent) hook that UNCONDITIONALLY injects a backoff+resume survival contract into every sub-agent dispatch via additionalContext — the mechanical enforcement of the cross-session resilience rule so future dispatches can't forget it. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_resilience_inject.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). On every Task/Agent spawn it emits a 5-clause verbatim contract: (1) BACKOFF — on rate-limit / 429 / 'overloaded' / throttle errors, do NOT abort, wait + retry with exponential backoff before treating as fatal; (2) CHECKPOINT — commit work in small logical units AS YOU GO (commit-early) so progress is durable, push when a unit completes; (3) RESUME — if restarted / re-dispatched, do NOT start over, inspect the last pushed commit and continue from there; (4) REPORT-ON-HALT — if you must stop before completion, report the last-good checkpoint (commit sha + what remains) so the parent can re-dispatch; (5) STEER — SendMessage is absent in this env, so do NOT TaskStop+relaunch to amend scope; run `/walkie on` early and the parent steers via `/walkie call <handle>` (sidecar walkie skill). The ONLY gate is `tool_name ∈ {Task, Agent}` — unlike plan-guard there is NO plan-file / marker / env-var / flag / config gating, the contract is injected on EVERY dispatch (commons @D s11 — no escape hatch; @D s7 — the hook IS the enforcement, no prose rule). Contract text is INLINE hardcoded (immutable constant), reads stdin via `exec("cat")` (hexa native, avoids fake-FILE segfault), portable `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths only. additionalContext only — NO updatedInput, NO permissionDecision — so it stacks safely alongside plan-guard + subagent-route at the same PreToolUse(Task|Agent) surface. Always exits 0. Mirrors the plan-guard inject-hook pattern (defensive readers · JSON-escaped additionalContext · no network). Known limit: additionalContext is advisory prompt steering, not a transport-layer retry — it shapes sub-agent behavior, it does not patch the API client.

plugins.name: drift-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that prevents design-drift regression — when a design-defining surface is edited but MEMORY.md + CLAUDE.md/project.tape are not, the next session (which loads memory at startup, not the diff) regresses to the old design. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_drift_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Fires on any of: (1) SSOT file edit — path ends in `.tape` or `/CLAUDE.md`; (2) a new artifact under a `decisions/` folder; (3) an explicit `@design-change` marker in the post-write content. Skips the mirror targets themselves (`/memory/`, MEMORY.md) and the guard's own install dir. Emits non-blocking systemMessage nudging the model to mirror the change into the project auto-memory (memory file + MEMORY.md pointer), or — for a cross-project governance rule — to ask the user to `sidecar sign` commons.tape/project.tape. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only. 0.1.1 — kills self-trigger noise: sentinel scan now limited to source-code extensions (.hexa/.py/.sh/…), so prose-heavy docs (marketplace/README/CHANGELOG/SKILL) that DOCUMENT the marker no longer self-trigger on every edit; sidecar repo's own /hooks/drift-guard/ tree is now skipped (in addition to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT cache). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: agents-tape-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `AGENTS.tape` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_agents_tape_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target whose basename is `AGENTS.tape` (any directory) is denied with a redirecting message — AGENTS.tape is a DORMANT carry (commons @D g16: don't touch, user-request override only), and the agent cannot distinguish a user-explicit override from a model-autonomous edit, so all AGENTS.tape writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. Enforces commons @D g16. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: changelog-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write) hard block for a `CHANGELOG.md` written anywhere other than a repo root, implemented in hexa-lang (`_changelog_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). commons @D g29: CHANGELOG.md is the chronological history surface at every repo ROOT (one per repo, at the top); a CHANGELOG.md buried in a plugin/source subdir fragments the history surface and violates s16 (co-locate by owner). Denies a Write when the basename is `CHANGELOG.md` AND the path contains a source-bucket segment (`/hooks/`, `/commands/`, `/skills/`); a repo-root CHANGELOG.md is allowed. Enforces commons @D g29. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: portable-path-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) GROWS TEETH (guard-teeth-sweep @L4 · s7) — promotes the detection from PostToolUse advisory to a PreToolUse(Write|Edit) HARD DENY: BEFORE the write lands, the guard reconstructs the proposed content from the payload (Write content · Edit old_string→new_string, replace_all aware, JSON-unescaped) and DENIES (`permissionDecision:"deny"`) when a shipped plugin script (a `.hexa`/`.sh`/`.py` file under `hooks/`/`commands/`/`skills/`) carries a hardcoded absolute home path literal (`/Users/` or `/home/`). A `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`/`$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` reference is NOT a `/Users/`/`/home/` literal so it passes; deterministic substring match, no opt-out (project.tape @D s11). The existing PostToolUse advisory is KEPT as a backstop for out-of-band edits. commons @D g13 (portable paths only), implemented in hexa-lang (`_portable_path_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A hardcoded path breaks the moment the plugin runs on another host/user — the deny names the literal + line and the portable form (`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` · `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` · `$HOME` · PATH binary). Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. PostToolUse path de-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. 0.1.1 PostToolUse advisory-only version. NO opt-out by design (s11).

plugins.name: version-lockstep-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) DEDUP/CLARITY: the advisory now explicitly DEFERS to the commit-gate. `sidecar-lint` already DENIES the `git commit` on marketplace<->plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22), so this guard is the EARLY edit-time heads-up ONLY — its additionalContext + header name sidecar-lint at commit-time as the HARD GATE so the two never double-fire confusingly. No second redundant deny added. PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for commons @D g22 (version discipline — bump SemVer + lockstep surfaces), in hexa-lang (invoked via `hexa run`). When a `plugin.json` version is written/edited, emits non-blocking additionalContext naming the plugin + new version and nudging the lockstep surfaces (matching marketplace.json entry + repo-root CHANGELOG.md, SAME commit), pointing at the sidecar-lint commit-gate. For an Edit fires only when the `version` field changed. Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only — does NOT auto-edit marketplace.json. NO opt-out by design (s11).

plugins.name: commons-s7-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory enforcing project.tape @D s7 (ship governance + its enforcement together) AT the commons.tape edit moment, implemented in hexa-lang (`_commons_s7_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When a write/edit ADDS one or more `@D <id>` rule blocks to `commons.tape`, the guard git-diffs the file, takes the IDs present on `+@D` lines but NOT on any `-@D` line (so a reworded existing rule is excluded — modification, not addition), enumerates the genuinely new rule IDs, and injects a non-blocking s7 checklist naming each new rule and demanding its guard/lint ship in the same cycle (clone an existing guard; register in marketplace.json + tier in profiles.json) — or, for a judgment-only rule, say so explicitly and keep commons.tape lean. This is the meta-rule the whole guard fleet exists to satisfy, now self-enforcing so rules can no longer land inert. Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. De-dupes per (session, new-rule-set). Advisory-only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: rolename-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) GROWS TEETH (guard-teeth-sweep @L4 · s7) — promotes the detection from PostToolUse advisory to a PreToolUse(Write) HARD DENY: BEFORE a NEW file is created, the guard DENIES (`permissionDecision:"deny"`) when its basename carries a g72 version/generation marker. Gated on the file NOT already existing — an existing (grandfathered) badly-named file's rewrite is NOT blocked (Edit always passes too; the matcher is Write-only), so the deny catches only the new-name moment. Filename-only, deterministic; no opt-out (project.tape @D s11). The existing PostToolUse advisory is KEPT as a backstop. commons @D g72 (minimal role-name identifiers — no version/generation marker), implemented in hexa-lang (`_rolename_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). MARKERS: versioned tails `_v<N>` · `_mk<N>` · `_c<N>` · `_gen<N>` · `_stage<N>`, word suffixes `_new` · `_old` · `_final`, or a bare `stage<N>` — version belongs in semver / git tag / metadata, never the name. FILENAME-SCOPED only (no content scan), so it never false-fires on ordinary in-body identifiers like `is_new` / `old_value`. Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. PostToolUse path de-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. 0.1.1 PostToolUse advisory-only version. NO opt-out by design (s11).

plugins.name: g70-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. UserPromptSubmit advisory enforcing commons @D g70 (non-Latin term canonical-command map · stall-prevention), implemented in hexa-lang (`_g70_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Watches the submitted prompt for the mapped KR-phonetic / legacy terms and injects the canonical command as non-blocking additionalContext so the model never grep-spins on a non-Latin lookup. Map: `bonghap` / `봉합` / `blue-max` / `블루맥스` → run `hexa verify --blue-max <D>` + flip `absorbed=true` (g69 BLUE-MAX 🔵-pair finalization); `NEXUS` / `넥서스` → RETIRED, use the repo-root `DOMAINS.tape` connection-graph SSOT (g67 · hub g68). Exact dictionary match (low false-positive). De-dupes once per (session, matched-set) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: skill-desc-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) GROWS TEETH (guard-teeth-sweep @L3 · s7) — adds a PreToolUse(Write) HARD DENY: BEFORE a SKILL.md write lands, the guard reconstructs the proposed `description:` from the payload `tool_input.content` (JSON-unescaped), measures it, and DENIES (`permissionDecision:"deny"`) when it exceeds the 1400-char Claude Code skill-listing cap, so an over-cap entry never gets written (the model can not silently ship a description that drops the skill from the listing). Deterministic char count only; no opt-out (project.tape @D s11). The existing PostToolUse(Write) advisory is KEPT as a backstop for out-of-band edits. project.tape @D s18 (a SKILL.md frontmatter description must stay <=1400 chars, the Claude Code per-entry skill-listing cap), implemented in hexa-lang (`_skill_desc_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Extracts the frontmatter `description:` value (single-line or indented block scalar, quotes/block-marker stripped); over the cap an over-cap description gets truncated/dropped from the skill listing, after which the model stops auto-recognizing the skill and the only lever left is raising skillListingBudgetFraction (~31k tok/session), the wrong fix. SKILL.md-SCOPED only (no body/prose scan); sibling of s6 (body = one @D block). Skips the guard's own tree + install cache. PostToolUse path de-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. 0.1.1 PostToolUse advisory-only version. NO opt-out by design (s11).

plugins.name: verdict-gate
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit) hard block + Stop advisory enforcing commons @D g73 (a verdict is EARNED by independent recompute, never self-judged — anti-fake-closure). Strict filename gate: fires ONLY when the target basename matches `H_*.md` or equals `result.json`. Layer (A) HARD DENY — a Write/Edit that asserts a verdict token (🔵 / 🟢 / SUPPORTED-FORMAL / SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL) in such a file, while the backing repo-root `.verdicts/<slug>/` (slug = `H_<slug>.md` stripped of `H_`+`.md`, or the containing dir name for result.json) holds no non-empty `*.txt` (verbatim `hexa verify` stdout), is denied with a redirect to run `hexa verify` and persist the output first. Repo root = nearest ancestor with a `.git`, else cwd. Layer (B) ADVISORY (non-blocking systemMessage) — a `.hexa` write containing a tautological smoke (a `check`'d `fn` whose single-statement body returns the literal/param it is compared against) warns 'tautological smoke — not verification (g73)'. Layer (C) Stop ADVISORY — if recent (mtime < 6h) `H_*.md` files assert 🔵/🟢 with 0% `.verdicts/` backing, emits a non-blocking Stop reason 'loop self-judging only · STOP & report (g73)'; never a hard stop-block (a hard block could trap an autonomous loop). Non-applicable tokens ⚪ SPECULATION-FENCED / 🟡 CITATION / 🟠 INSUFFICIENT NEVER fire (already honest). Plain `.md` prose mentioning 🔵 (the RFC, commons.tape, README rubric) is NOT caught — the filename gate excludes it. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_verdict_gate.hexa` + `_verdict_stop.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the standing commons @D s7 gap (g73 landed without its enforcement hook). NO opt-out by design (commons @D s11 — gated only on real preconditions: missing payload skip, filename gate, token absent skip, evidence present skip).

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: 0.14.0 (2026-06-06) PROMOTED two highest-confidence, zero-false-positive deterministic checks from advisory to HARD COMMIT-BLOCKING DENY (permissionDecision deny on the `git commit`): (a) marketplace.json<->plugin.json version DRIFT (exact mismatch, commons @D g22) and (b) a literal `/Users/`·`/home/` absolute path in a plugin SCRIPT being committed (.hexa/.sh/.py under hooks|commands|skills, non-comment line · g13/s3). The deny scope is narrow so it never over-blocks: drift = exact string compare; abs-path = SCRIPT-ONLY + comment-skip (a .md/.json/.tape citing a path stays ADVISORY). Verified zero false-positives vs this repo's real history before promoting. ALL other ~9 checks stay advisory; version-lockstep-guard 0.2.0 defers to this commit-gate. 0.13.0 (2026-06-02) REMOVED check #7 (STDIO MCP-server ban) — project.tape @D s15 was RETIRED (PR #382), so the lint check retires together with the rule + the stdio-mcp-guard (s7 symmetry). STDIO and HTTP/SSE MCP-server plugins are now BOTH permitted (`agentmemory-mcp` is the first); the lint no longer flags either. PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in repos with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Mostly advisory — emits additionalContext for: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15), hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths (commons @D g13 · sidecar @D s3), marketplace.json plugin entry ↔ each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22), hook .sh missing user-exec bit, missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29), and — when the repo carries .claude-plugin/profiles.json (sidecar's multi-user enable profiles) — any marketplace plugin missing a tier there (sidecar @D s7: an untagged plugin silently defaults to 'personal', hidden from the minimal/hexa profiles), and any registered STDIO MCP-server plugin (project.tape @D s15 — `mcps/` source or command-based `mcpServers`; STDIO MCP forbidden because its stdin/stdout JSON-RPC handshake collides with hexa hook stdio + fake-stdin; an HTTP/SSE-transport `mcpServers` carrying a `url` is PERMITTED and not flagged). 0.10.0 — added a SKILL.md s6 check (project.tape @D s6): a `skills/*/SKILL.md` body (after frontmatter) is flagged when it holds >1 `@D` block, prose/heading/table/code-fence beyond do/dont, a @D key other than do/dont, or a per-skill `README.md` exists — closing the s7 enforcement gap (s6 was required-active with no lint). 0.9.0 — s15 relaxed to allow HTTP/SSE MCP; the MCP check now flags only stdio (url/http/sse marker present → allowed). 0.8.1 — the top-level-dir check (project.tape @D s16: co-locate by owner/domain) is now an ALLOWLIST, not a name-blocklist: any TRACKED top-level dir that is NOT a concept dir (`hooks`·`commands`·`skills`·`agents`) / `bin` / `.github` / `.claude-plugin` is flagged, so renaming a bucket (`docs`→`documentation`, `state`→`tmp`) does NOT dodge it. The advisory directs co-location under the owning domain/plugin. Tracked-only (`git ls-files`), so gitignored runtime/scratch (`state/`·`drafts/`·`build/`) never false-fires. Self-excludes its own files + CHANGELOG.md. NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list. 0.11.0 — added a `!`-exec parse-safety check (check 10): each `*/commands/*.md` is flagged when its `!`backtick-exec block holds an unescaped inner backtick (a bare backtick prematurely closes the command → zsh parse error near newline, the slash verb dies). A well-formed block carries exactly TWO unescaped backticks (opener + closer); backslash-escaped or single-quoted alternatives are safe. Caught the real walkie `/walkie handle` breakage. 0.12.0 — added a TOOLKIT.jsonl staleness check (check 11 · sidecar @D s17): a marketplace-registered plugin whose `"id":"<name>"` is absent from the root `TOOLKIT.jsonl` capability catalog is flagged (a forgotten `bin/_gen_toolkit.hexa` regen after shipping a capability); only checked when TOOLKIT.jsonl exists.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: 0.9.0 (2026-06-02) SEVERITY DIAL — `force` / `warn` / `off`, read from the enclosing project's `project.tape` `@C hexa-native` block (`mode = "..."`): force = hard-deny (prior behavior · default when the dial is absent/unknown/unreadable, fail-safe to strictest), warn = ALLOW + non-blocking `systemMessage` note steering to `.hexa`, off = silent pass-through. The dial lives in the sign-gated SSOT (project.tape @D s13), so LOWERING severity needs a fresh user `sidecar sign project` — it can NOT auto-flip to silence, which is why it is NOT the env-var/config opt-out @D s11 forbids (s11 carries the explicit EXCEPTION); the guard body stays unconditional, only the sign-gated dial selects severity. 0.8.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` / `.c` / `.cc` / `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h` / `.hpp` / `.hh` / `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` / `.s` / `.S` / `.asm` / `.o` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.8.0 adds C++ inline/template headers `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` + preprocessed-asm `.S` (uppercase) + `.asm` (0.7.0 `.hpp` / `.hh`; 0.6.0 `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h`; 0.5.0 `.cc` / `.s` / `.o`; 0.4.0 `.c`; on top of `.py` / `.sh`) — a hexa-native project takes `.hexa` only; the full hand-written C / C++ / header / inline / asm / object family is refused (regenerated via `hexa cc --regen`, never hand-edited per commons @D g61); each suffix matches literally + case-sensitively (`.S`≠`.s`, `.h`≠`.sh`/`.hexa`, `.o`≠`.go`/`.io`). Covers BOTH write channels: structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash write channels — `> X.inl`, `>> X.S`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), `tee [-a] X`, plus non-redirect channels `dd of=X` and `cp`/`mv` destination (0.3.2); relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware redirects; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.c`, `gcc -c X.c -o X.o`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through (compiler `-o` output is NOT a covered channel). Unbounded channels (`python -c open(...)`, `sed -i`, `perl -e`, `install -t`) are NOT covered — this narrows the common write paths, not a sandbox. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with an action-first message that leads with the fix (re-issue as `.hexa`) and demotes the refusal reason. NO env-var / flag opt-out: the only severity control is the sign-gated `project.tape` dial (0.9.0, above) — no env var, no out-of-band config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion; or uninstall the plugin. Targets only `.py` / `.sh` / `.c` / `.cc` / `.cpp` / `.cxx` / `.h` / `.hpp` / `.hh` / `.inl` / `.ipp` / `.tcc` / `.s` / `.S` / `.asm` / `.o`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: sign-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.2 (2026-06-01) drops the stale `sidecar sign local` example from the self-mint doc comment now that the pool-route force-local `local` key is removed (pool-route 0.20.0); governance keys commons/project/gitignore/paths unchanged. 0.2.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files — currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review), and `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (0.1.7 — the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST: a registered path-prefix silently overrides pool-route to force commands LOCAL, so a stealth add routes arbitrary commands off the pool) — may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` · `project` · `gitignore` · `paths`), writing the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools and the Bash write channels (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected — only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash · Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command invocation: the Bash channel is scanned on a quote-masked copy and sidecar must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator), so a sidecar sign <key> MENTION inside a quoted commit message or PR body is no longer mis-flagged - pre-0.1.5 it wrongly blocked legit commits that referenced the command. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely — the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) defeating hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, not an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). REACTIVE ff-only stale-base auto-sync (0.2.0): before ALLOWING a signed edit to a gated SSOT file, `safe_ff_sync(<file>)` TRANSPARENTLY fast-forwards JUST that file to origin/<default> (`git checkout origin/<default> -- <file>`) so the edit lands on a FRESH base — the 2026-05-30 stale-base accident (a 28-commit-behind project.tape edit that would have REVERTED origin governance) can't start. SAFETY RAILS: ff-only (NEVER merge-commit/rebase/force) · clean-path-only · skip-on-doubt (no origin · detached · mid-rebase/merge · untracked · identical) · FETCH_HEAD-gated fetch (>5min). NEVER blocks the edit: synced/skipped are SILENT, a dirty/diverged path prints EXACTLY ONE advisory line + STILL allows. To gate another file: add one row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.

plugins.name: plist-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.3 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.plist` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_plist_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target file ending in `.plist` (LaunchAgents · LaunchDaemons · Info.plist · UserDefaults) is denied with a redirecting message — the agent cannot distinguish user-explicit from model-autonomous intent, so all .plist writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. LaunchAgents / LaunchDaemons are persistence surfaces (auto-launch on login / boot), so silent writes risk installing daemons the user did not ask for. Enforces commons @D g37 (plist user-request only). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: handoff-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-05) DENY scope BROADENED + NEW SessionStart INJECT hook. The per-repo `inbox/` folder pattern is RETIRED ecosystem-wide (hexa-lang PR #2728), so handoffs route through the SINGLE `sidecar handoff` registry (`~/.sidecar/handoff/handoff.jsonl`), NEVER scattered `.md` / per-repo `inbox/` markdown. (A) PreToolUse(Write·Edit·MultiEdit) HARD-DENY now bites a file-write whose `file_path` (a) contains `/inbox/` at ANY depth AND ends in `.md` (the whole retired inbox-markdown scatter — `inbox/patches/*.md`, `inbox/anything.md`, deep-nested `…/inbox/…/*.md`), OR (b) has basename in {`HANDOFF.md`, `INBOX.md`}. The `.md`-scope on the `/inbox/` match is the deliberate false-positive guard — a legit app's `inbox/queue.json` / `inbox/handler.py` is NOT blocked; only markdown handoff-scatter bites. Redirects: cross-repo gap/patch/bug/RFC → `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text...>` (+ `ls` / `done <id>`); next-session intra-repo dossier → fold into the EPHEMERAL `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` `## handoff` section (gitignored · s16). (B) NEW SessionStart INJECT hook surfaces OPEN handoffs destined for the CURRENT repo (resolved from `git remote get-url origin` basename, else cwd basename) into the session — reads the JSONL SSOT for OPEN items whose `to` == current repo and emits an additionalContext `📥 open handoffs for <repo> (N): <id · text> …` line so an unprocessed cross-repo handoff is never forgotten; SILENT exit 0 on zero open items or any error (never spams a clean session). Both hooks fail-open (compile/runtime error → exit 0). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_handoff_guard.hexa` deny · `_handoff_inject.hexa` inject, invoked via `hexa run`; graceful exit 0 when hexa absent). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11). Deferred follow-up: `sidecar handoff` CLI hardening (empty-text reject / dedup) — out of scope here.

plugins.name: secret-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) ADVISORY hook enforcing commons @D g77 (credential exists-check before register / login), in hexa-lang (`_secret_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Seals the recurring 'credential is missing / please register' anti-pattern when the key ACTUALLY EXISTS in the `secret` store (the 2026-05-30 HF token incident: an agent saw a `huggingface.token` 401 and told the user to register, but `hf.token` held a valid token). Two detectors consult `secret list` (key NAMES only — values NEVER read/printed): (a) `secret set <K>` where <K> already exists → advises read via `secret get <K>`, overwrite only if rotating; (b) credential-acquisition commands `hf auth login` / `huggingface-cli login` / `gh auth login` / `aws configure` / `<tool> login` when a plausibly-corresponding key EXISTS (service→key heuristic: hf/huggingface → `hf`/`hugg`; gh/github → `github`/`gh`; aws → `aws`), surfacing the matching key names + a known-alias hint (huggingface.token ↔ hf.token). ADVISORY ONLY — `permissionDecision:"allow"` + `additionalContext`, NEVER deny (commons @D g5 / guards-narrow-scope: must not block legitimate re-registration / rotation); mirrors git-guard's non-blocking emit. CONSERVATIVE — fires only on a real match; `secret list` / `secret get` / `--help` / bare reads pass SILENTLY. Shell separators normalized before tokenizing. FAILS OPEN on any error. NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11).

plugins.name: deck-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for hand-authoring a `.in` input deck — forces the `/deck` generator (canonical `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` SSOT). ALL writes whose target basename ends in `.in` / `.in.head` / `.in.tail` are denied + redirected to `/deck`, EXCEPT a WHITELIST of build-system templates: Makefile.in / makefile.in / GNUmakefile.in / configure.in / config*.in (autoconf incl. config.h.in) · *.pc.in · *.spec.in · Doxyfile.in · *.pro.in, PLUS (Write/Edit) content carrying an autoconf `@VAR@` substitution marker (generic autoconf template, not a QE/DFT deck). Two channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured tools (file_path) + Bash (`>`/`>>` · heredoc · tee · dd of= · cp/mv dest; Bash uses basename whitelist, @VAR@ content-whitelist applies to Write/Edit). hexa-lang (`_deck_guard.hexa`). The whitelist is a SCOPE precondition (build templates are not decks), not a disable switch — NO opt-out by design (@D s11).

plugins.name: ai-api-guard
plugins.description: 0.3.0 (2026-06-06) s14 capacity-relief PROMOTED to a hard deny on the Bash path: a Bash command that SETS/EXPORTS `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (`export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-… cmd`, `setenv`, or `--api-key`+anthropic) as a Claude rate-limit/capacity workaround now `permissionDecision:"deny"` — gated on an actual assignment/export precondition, so a MERE mention (`echo`/`grep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, no `=`/export) stays ALLOW (no false-positive block). Write/Edit content stays ADVISORY (additionalContext nudge) since a config/env mention can be legitimate. Both redirect to the SANCTIONED CLI routes (account swap via `/quota` · CLI routers · Bedrock/Vertex backends) per project.tape @D s14. PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). g50 deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` · `api.anthropic.com` · `fal.run` · `api.replicate.com` · `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. — commons @D g50 enforcement. Whitespace/line-continuation normalized before matching; dynamic imports out of scope (static scan). General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. matcher Bash|Write|Edit. NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11).

plugins.name: verify-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.4 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw verification-tool usage cited as primary evidence, implemented in hexa-lang (`_verify_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Denies first-token ∈ {wolframscript, mathematica, WolframKernel}, and inline-Python verification via `python(3)? -c '...'` containing `import sympy` / `from sympy` / `import pyphi` / `import wolframclient` with a redirecting message pointing to `hexa verify` (commons @D g5). General sympy / wolframclient usage in committed code files passes through — only inline `-c` verification probes (and the standalone Wolfram CLIs) are refused. 0.1.3 — dead retired-rule source citation in plugin.json replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pr-cycle
plugins.description: /pr-cycle — one-shot PR cycle command. Pushes the current branch (`git push -u origin HEAD`), `gh pr create --fill`, SELF-MERGES with `gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch`, then SWEEPS merged agent worktrees in the SAME command block — one command does create → merge → clean. Refuses on main/master. Pass extra gh flags as args. 0.5.0 — POST-MERGE WORKTREE SWEEP closes the leak where `--delete-branch` removed the remote branch but left the `.claude/worktrees/agent-*` DIRECTORY piling up across /cycle-bg runs. After a successful merge the command cd's to the MAIN worktree and removes every linked agent worktree whose upstream is `[gone]` (the squash-safe 'merged + branch deleted' signal — `--is-ancestor` cannot detect a squash merge, but `git push -u` then `--delete-branch` reliably leaves the upstream gone), plus `git branch -D` + `git worktree prune`. cd-to-main first means even the just-merged CURRENT worktree (when /pr-cycle ran inside one) is swept. SAFETY: never touches a worktree with a live OR absent (`track=none`) upstream — those may hold un-pushed work — nor `locked` worktrees (another checkout); only paths under `/.claude/worktrees/` are eligible. 0.4.1 — command appends the merge ITSELF instead of relying on the `pr-cycle-hook` PreToolUse hook: slash-command `!`-exec does NOT route through the Bash-tool PreToolUse hook, so the hook never fired for /pr-cycle (PRs left created-but-unmerged); self-merge fixes it, idempotent with the hook (skips any command already containing `gh pr merge`). Command half of the pr-cycle split (routing hook = `pr-cycle-hook`, still serves agent Bash-tool `gh pr create`).

plugins.name: pr-cycle-hook
plugins.description: 0.3.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Quote-aware tokenizer scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere — catches bare and chained (`git push && gh pr create …`) forms. When matched (not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED or the rewrite is ignored) to append the merge tail and, IF cwd is a linked worktree, ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force; git branch -D <branch>)` — atomic create → merge → worktree+branch cleanup. Cross-repo `--repo X` routes the merge to X, skips local cleanup. 0.3.0 — explicit PR# capture via `gh pr view --json number -q .number` (cross-repo via `gh pr view --repo X`); robust when branch-ref is detached / worktree mid-teardown / CI strips head-ref; cross-repo pins both view+merge to the target repo. DELETION-SANITY GATE (0.2.0, anima #1105): before appending the auto-merge, probes the squash payload (`git diff --name-status <merge-base(origin/main,HEAD)>...HEAD`, or `gh pr diff` cross-repo) for a mass-deletion outlier — D-count > 50, OR deletions >= 10x additions and > 20 abs — and DENIES the merge (PR stays created, the dangerous squash-merge is withheld) to block stale-base regressions like the 35190-file revert-delete. Fail-open on probe failure. Enforces commons @D g47. Skip: `--draft` · already contains `gh pr merge`. Hook half of the pr-cycle split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: 0.5.4 (2026-06-02) fields rule adds the pre-registered-hypothesis family (seed · claim · falsifier · axes · target · honest) to the non-governance structured-field set, in lockstep with hexa-lang `tape_d_field_allowed` (PR #2492). Only `:: governance` `@D` blocks stay strict (do / dont only); non-governance `@D` kinds (workflow · domain · config · note · hypothesis · spec · identity · ...) may also carry a documented structured-field set (version · kind · brief · parent · ssot · siblings · ref · refs · note · clarify · scope · mode · why · tool · ex · see · seed · claim · falsifier · axes · target · honest). An undocumented field, or ANY extra field on a governance block, is still refused (drift guard). Kept in lockstep with the hexa-lang `.tape` grammar's allowed field list. PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` — no Python, no shell shim). hooks.json hexa-run wrapper fails open (compile/runtime error exits 0; guard deny exit 2 preserved). Four checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule — see above; fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap — `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language — when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul · CJK · Hiragana · Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. (4) @I siblings — project.tape's @I block must carry a `siblings` field; dropping it or adding a fresh @I without it is refused (cross-repo network SSOT). project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: 0.24.0 (2026-06-02) FREEZE-DEFENSE (Gap A+B · mini kernel-panic): _local_hexa_count() now matches the `hexa_run.` basename prefix (Gap B — the storm was hexa_run.* compiled-run binaries, not basename==hexa) · LOCAL heavy/whitelist hexa-exec rewritten with a per-process `ulimit -v <KB>` (=55% RAM · RLIMIT_AS · Gap A · ADVISORY on macOS where ulimit -v does not bind — smoke-confirmed — load-bearing on a Linux pool host) · LIVE fork-storm admission DENY on the default-LOCAL heavy hexa-exec path when live hexa+hexa_run.* count ≥ FORK_STORM_CAP. Enforcing layer for the Mac freeze is the companion `sidecar fuse` freeze-imminent SIGKILL tier. Proposed governance s19 staged in the plan (sign-gated). 0.23.0 (2026-06-01) MEM-PRESSURE ADMISSION GUARD (freeze-safety · sibling of FORK_STORM_CAP): mini (Mac mini pool host) froze AGAIN — a heavy hexa-exec ran LOCAL (auto-dispatch removed since 0.21.0), ballooned RSS → system mem ~99% → kernel page-thrash → sshd starved → SSH banner never sent. 0.22.0 only NUDGES (advisory); if the agent runs the heavy cmd anyway, the Mac freezes. 0.23.0 adds a DENY admission guard at the heavy advisory exit: when ALL hold — heavy AND `_is_hexa_exec(toks)` (the pool-eligible killer class; swift/xcode can't go to a linux pool), live `_mac_mem_pct() >= MEM_ADMIT_PCT` (=88; mirrors `sidecar fuse` fuse_mem_pct() via vm_stat, fail-OPEN on -1), AND an eligible NON-macos host exists in h_name/h_plat — the command is DENIED with a prefilled `sidecar pool on <linux-host> <cmd>` runbook + `sidecar fuse enforce` retry hint. This is an ADMISSION gate, NOT a revived auto-route (project.tape @D s10 intact — no ssh-rewrite, no forced peer dispatch), with NO opt-out var (project.tape @D s11). New `_mac_mem_pct()` helper kept in lockstep with `sidecar fuse` mem-reap (reactive) — this is the proactive admission half. SessionStart policy preamble amended: pool-deny is now admission-only (honest). 0.22.0 (2026-06-01) HEAVY→POOL ADVISORY NUDGE (s12 education · user feature): 0.21.0's auto-dispatch removal left ZERO guidance to use the pool for heavy work. The early unconditional `_allow()` is REMOVED so flow continues through the existing local-bound / whitelist guards + the heavy classifier; at a new ADVISORY-ONLY exit (right after `heavy` is computed, BEFORE any host-selection / routing / deny) a HEAVY command (build / test / compile / hexa-exec / large-tree find) emits a one-line `additionalContext` nudge — `sidecar pool on <linux-host> <cmd>` for general-heavy, a macOS pool host for swift/xcode (project.tape @D s12) — then is ALWAYS allowed LOCAL. Re-satisfies both 0.21.0 rationales: a box with NO pool roster (`len(h_name)==0`) exits silently local with NO nudge, and we exit at the advisory and NEVER reach routing/deny so nothing ships to a peer. Local-bound / whitelist work (git · gh · pool · npm · $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT · hexa cloud · atlas register · `sidecar paths` whitelist · local-data-input) exits above with NO nudge. NO deny, NO rewrite (s10 — still local-by-default, pool EXPLICIT-only), NO opt-out var (s11). New `heavy_local` tally counter; new `_heavy_pool_advisory_text()` mirrors the `_pool_advisory_text()` pattern; routing machinery stays dead+retained. 0.21.0 (2026-06-01) AUTO-DISPATCH DISABLED — the hook no longer rewrites a Bash command into `ssh <pool-host>` nor denies it toward a pool; every command runs LOCAL by default and pool is explicit-only via `sidecar pool on <host> <cmd>`. Removes the 0.9.0 default-POOL inversion that broke fresh self-hosted boxes (no roster) and silently shipped local-bound work (local-fs builds, /tmp outputs) to peers. The classifier is retained (dead) for a reviewed re-enable; SessionStart routing-log + the policy preamble (now stating local-by-default) still run. 0.20.0 (2026-06-01) removes the redundant `sidecar sign local` force-all-local toggle — the roster-arm gate already runs everything local when ~/.pool/pool.json is empty and routes heavy work to the pool when it is not, and the `sidecar paths` whitelist covers the deliberate per-prefix force-local case; dropped `_local_signed()` + `LOCAL_SIGN_TTL` + `_local_heavy_interp()` + the abs-path heavy sign-gate (abs-path/dotstate now pin local unconditionally) + the injected-policy `sign local` line. Governance `sidecar sign project|commons` unaffected. 0.19.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. 0.19.0 SIGN-LOCAL PERSISTENT TOGGLE (user feature 2026-06-01): `_local_signed()` content-types `~/.sidecar/signs/local.sign` — content `on` = PERSISTENT force-local (never expires · `sidecar sign local on`, clear with `off`) · numeric epoch = the LEGACY 30-min bang (`! sidecar sign local`, unchanged) · absent = OFF (the default). An explicit user-owned on/off toggle (default off) alongside the time-boxed bang, like the `pool mini-equal on` opt-in (s12) — a user state toggle, NOT an agent-flippable opt-out of a guard (s11): sign-guard keeps DENYING the agent from minting EITHER value. bin/sidecar gains `sign local on|off` verbs + a non-numeric arithmetic guard in `sign --list` (shows `ON (persistent)`). 0.18.0 HEXA SOURCE-ROOT FIX: a routed `hexa <verb>` now pins HEXA_LANG AND HEXA_MODULE_LOADER at the hexa-lang checkout `$HOME/core/hexa-lang` for EVERY hexa verb, not just kick/drill/loop/cc. The 0.5.5 env passthrough defaulted both to `$PWD` (the SYNCED workdir the route lands in — `~/core/sidecar`/`~/core/anima`); the 0.6.x `hexa_subverb` whitelist only redirected kick/drill/loop/cc to the checkout. Every OTHER routed verb (atlas · verify · run <stdlib script> · …) mis-pointed its source root at the synced workdir, which has NO `compiler/`/stdlib root (sidecar) and where `$PWD/build/hexa_module_loader` does not even exist (while `$HOME/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_module_loader` does on every host) → `absorbed verb '...' script not found`. The `cd <workdir>` target is UNCHANGED (relative paths still land in the synced workdir); only the env source-root pointer is corrected. Env is set ONLY on the routed remote command string — sidecar's OWN hexa-native / pool-route guard hooks invoke `hexa run "$@"` directly and never read these vars, so they are unaffected (verified: hexa-native still denies a `.py` write post-fix). The per-verb `hexa_subverb` split is removed. 0.17.0 FIND HOST-INTROSPECTION: `find` joins the host-introspection LOCAL-EXECUTION whitelist (alongside du/df/ps/lsof/…) — it walks the LOCAL filesystem tree, so routing it to a pool host returns the PEER's tree (wrong for a disk survey / file search on this Mac). The 0.9.0 launch deliberately excluded `find` to spare the workstation a heavy `find /` scan, but that conflated 'heavy' with 'portable': a local-fs-bound command must stay local regardless of CPU cost (@D s10 — route on portability, never on load). First-token basename match; no opt-out (@D s11). 0.16.0 DECK-GEN PIN BROADENED: the deck local-pin match widened from the single absolute path-string `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` to IDENTITY-based — also catches a relative `deck/gen.hexa` form (run from inside the stdlib dir / a symlinked cwd) AND a future `hexa deck` verb form, so NO deck-generator invocation path can slip to a pool resource (bypass the local pin); the cloud pin already matches `hexa cloud` adjacency so it needs no broadening. 0.15.0 DECK-GEN LOCAL PIN (s3 companion): a 6th structural LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption pins any command referencing `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` to LOCAL — the `/deck` runnable became `$HOME/.hx/install/hexa-lang/stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` (s3 portability) so it no longer carries a `/Users/` literal; without the pin it would route to a Linux pool host where that Mac-local hx artifact is absent and break.
