Mirrors the cloud/atlas pins; unconditional, no opt-out (@D s11). 0.14.0 SIDECAR CLI LOCAL-BOUND (routing bug fix): the bare `sidecar <verb>` CLI joins the local-bound early-exit whitelist alongside `git`/`gh`/`pool` (`_has_word(cmd, "sidecar")`). The `sidecar` marketplace CLI is host-LOCAL — it manages `~/.sidecar/` (handoff registry · signs · local-paths whitelist · profiles) + `~/.claude/` (installed_plugins.json · enabledPlugins) — so `sidecar handoff add …` / `sidecar sync` mis-routed to a pool host (ubu-2) wrote the WRONG machine's state (or 127'd where no `sidecar` binary exists). The bare CLI was NOT covered pre-fix: the $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA literal pin (0.6.8) + the `~/.sidecar` dotstate pin (0.6.9) only catch commands literally carrying those tokens, and `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>` carries neither. Mirrors the `pool` pin exactly (sidecar-family local CLI · @D s10 local-bound cmds stay local). 0.13.0 MINI-EQUAL TOGGLE DROPPED (user feature 2026-05-28): the `~/.pool/mini-equal` marker is no longer read — general-heavy round-robin is permanently Linux-only (Mac stays the workstation per @D s12). macOS pool hosts (mini) still receive macOS-only auto-dispatches (swift / xcodebuild / codesign / …); explicit `pool on mini <cmd>` is the manual escape for ad-hoc macOS work. Companion ship: pool 0.8.10 (drops `pool mini-equal` + `pool clean on|off` verbs + the `clean` per-host field). Together with 0.12.0 this retires the last on/off-style toggles in the pool stack. 0.12.0 ALWAYS-ON ROSTER (user feature 2026-05-28): the per-host `enabled` field is no longer read — every roster host listed in `~/.pool/pool.json` is treated as on for routing-candidate purposes. Drops the residual `enabled=true` filter at the head of the eligibility scan; the existing pi5-akida name-substring exclusion (anima-only · @D s8) is the ONLY remaining gate, so behavior is identical for the current roster (mini · ubu-1 · ubu-2 routing-eligible, pi5-akida excluded) but the model and `pool list` are no longer cluttered with an on/off toggle that never actually toggled. Companion ships: pool 0.8.9 (drops `shared:false` field + `[ded]` marker · akida exclusion via the same name-substring helper) + sidecar commons 0.11.0 (SessionStart roster snapshot renders `name[os]` instead of `name[os,on|off]`). 0.11.0 FORK-STORM CAP (user feature 2026-05-28): a whitelist-forced-LOCAL hexa run escapes the default hexa→pool offload and runs on the Mac; when FORK_STORM_CAP (default 8) or more USER hexa interpreters are already live locally, a NEW whitelist-local hexa is DENIED with a runbook — locality preserved (imports still resolve), storm capped. Counts via `ps -Ao args` (first-token basename `hexa`, one line per run), excludes sidecar's own hooks (`cache/sidecar`) + `claude` (@D s9), fails OPEN on a count error so it never blocks (@D s11). 0.10.0 SIDECAR PATH-PREFIX WHITELIST (user feature — chat-form sbs FULL agreement 2026-05-28): a new permanent LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption layer reads `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (line-based plain text, USER-managed via the new `sidecar paths {bare|add|rm}` CLI verb) — any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed absolute-path token that starts with a registered prefix is FORCED LOCAL (skip pool routing). PERMANENT counterpart to the runtime `sign-local` token (which is one-shot 30-min); no TTL. The hook scans ALL argv tokens, tolerates a missing file (empty whitelist · no fail-closed · no caching since the file is typically 2-10 lines), and skips blank + `#` comment lines. Seeded on first use by `sidecar paths` with sidecar's own cache + repo paths (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/`) so sidecar's own hexa hooks/commands stop being routed to the pool — closes the recurring fail mode where the cache (workstation-only) was sent to a pool peer. Sits between host-introspection and the heavy classifier in the routing decision; emits a one-line `pool-route: local (sidecar path whitelist · prefix=<matched>)` on hit. The whitelist itself IS the opt-in — no env opt-out (project.tape @D s11). Companion CLI: `sidecar paths` lists, `sidecar paths add` registers cwd, `sidecar paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `sidecar paths rm <prefix>` removes a line (refuses if not found). 0.9.3 DATA-LOCALITY PIN + SIGN-LOCAL TTL 30min (anima PURE F-CURRICULA-1): a local-data-dependent `hexa run` (Mac-local corpus input) was blocked on BOTH routing paths. (a) data-locality pin (5th structural LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption): a hexa exec whose data-INPUT flag (--corpus-path/--corpus/--data/--data-path/--dataset/--input/--train-data/--eval-data/--weights/--ckpt/--checkpoint) points at a LOCAL-ONLY path (anything NOT under a synced ~/core/ workdir) now PINS LOCAL — the pool peer has no copy of that data, so routing reproduced the observed failure (`hexa run ... --corpus-path /Users/.../c.jsonl` to ubu-2, compile-stage `source not found` = a data-absence error misread as a code error). Synced ~/core data still routes (peer mirrors it). Same data-locality necessity as the host-introspection whitelist; quote-stripped tokens; unconditional, no opt-out env (s11). (b) sign-local TTL raised 5min to 30min (LOCAL_SIGN_TTL=1800 in _local_signed()): a single legitimate local build (corpus regen + compile) runs 10-20min, exceeding the old 5min `! sidecar sign local` window, so a mid-build `hexa run` re-tripped the fork-storm sign gate on token expiry (re-sign every 5min was unworkable; F-CURRICULA-1 fire BLOCKED twice). TRADE-OFF: the fork-storm guard window is now 6x longer, accepted (the local token is explicit, user-minted with no agent self-mint, single-key, clearable early via `sidecar sign clear local`); a build-covering window is the Occam fix (g0) vs a sliding-window/session-marker mechanism. The local token TTL is INDEPENDENT of the governance sign TTL (SIGN_TTL=300 in bin/sidecar + hooks/sign-guard, gating agent EDITS to commons/project/gitignore, stays 5min); bin/sidecar mint message + --list are now key-aware (sign_ttl_for: local=1800, governance=300). 0.9.1 ROLLBACK of 0.8.3 canon: each pool host's hexa binary has host-specific path-resolution (ubu-1 = bash wrapper exec'ing hexa_real; ubu-2 = ELF binary with argv[0]-relative `self/native/hexat` search · symlinks were Mac-paths broken on Linux). HEXA_REAL_BIN/HEXA_LANG=canon injection works on some hosts, silently breaks on others (qwen_bpe.hexa `run` needs `self/native/hexat` at a host-specific path canon doesn't supply consistently). 0.9.1 reverts to host-owns-its-hexa: pool-route uses host's `~/core/hexa-lang` for `HEXA_LANG` and host's PATH-resolved `hexa` binary. Stale-stdlib is the host owner's responsibility (anima for ~/core/hexa-lang drift on ubu-2). `~/.hx/canon` + canon-update.sh remain as scaffolding on hosts where they were bootstrapped; pool-route no longer routes through canon. POLICY INVERSION (0.9.0 — 유저 directive "무조건 pool · 화이트리스트만 local 가능"): default = POOL. Only LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST hits stay local — heavy-vs-light gating + default-local fallthrough REMOVED. Whitelist (closed list, sign-gated additions): sidecar self-hooks ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA) · hexa cloud · hexa atlas register · git/gh · /Users//home abs-path heavy-interp (sign-gated) · ~/. dotstate · npm/pnpm/yarn · pool CLI · HOST-INTROSPECTION (0.9.0 new — ps · top · uptime · w · who · df · du · free · vmstat · iostat · uname · sysctl · launchctl · systemctl · pgrep · pkill · kill · killall · stat · lsof · netstat · ifconfig · ip · arp · route · dscl · scutil · sw_vers · first-token basename match · semantic-necessity not convenience) · `! sidecar sign local` 5min token. Pre-0.9.0 heavy classifier retained for OS-capability hints only (is_macos/is_linux → which pool host). The `find /` root-tree case (anima 100%+ CPU 2:44+) now routes by default. SSH overhead accepted. TERMINOLOGY (0.8.4): 'WHITELIST' = LOCAL-EXECUTION allowlist (things that escape pool dispatch · stay local) — the 0.8.0 'HEXA WHITELIST' phrasing was misleading and the rule was renamed to HEXA POOL-DISPATCH. Real whitelist members: $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA · hexa cloud · hexa atlas register · /Users//home abs-path heavy interp (sign-gated) · ~/. dotstate · git/gh · npm/pnpm/yarn · 5min `! sidecar sign local` runtime token. Registration onto the whitelist is sign-gated by design (commons @D s13 for hardcoded structural exemptions via project.tape · TUI `! sidecar sign local` for runtime force-local · no agent self-mint path). No env-var opt-out (commons @D s11). Pool-canonical hexa routing (0.8.3): pool dispatches pin hexa to `~/.hx/canon` (pool-owned origin/main checkout, independent of each host's `~/core/hexa-lang` which may carry feature branches / behind state / anima WIP). Wrapper-injection via `HEXA_REAL_BIN=$HOME/.hx/canon/build/hexa_linux` + `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/.hx/canon` + `HEXA_DAEMON=0`. Bootstrap / refresh via `~/.hx/canon-update.sh` (idempotent: git fetch origin main + reset --hard + copy bootstrap C from ~/core/hexa-lang + hexa cc --regen). Solves the stale-compiler / qwen_bpe miscompile class (ubu-2 was 614 commits behind on stale main + DNS-broken → fetch impossible; canon is independent of that drift). Probe: canon binary exists + wrapper executes `hexa --help` through canon. Hosts missing canon fail preflight with a runbook deny-trailer. HEXA WHITELIST (0.8.0 — POOL-OFFLOAD m8): ANY hexa execution (hexa/hexa.real/hexac/hexadrv/hxv2 as the real first-token after env-prefix+wrapper+backslash unwrap) routes to the pool — full stop, replacing the heavy-VERB blacklist that leaked verify/parse/atlas/honesty → multi-hundred-process Mac fork-storm. Only 4 STRUCTURAL exemptions stay local (handled earlier, never reach the whitelist): $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA hexa (sidecar's own hooks — routing the router is incoherent), `hexa cloud` (itself a dispatcher), /Users//home/~/. abs-path hexa (sign-gated, path may be absent on peer), and `hexa atlas register` (0.8.1 — mutates local SSOT embedded.gen.hexa + commits locally; routing breaks the share workflow — only the register WRITE subverb, read forms still route). The legacy heavy_words/heavy_pairs classifier remains for NON-hexa heavy compute (make/cargo/gcc/node/java/ffmpeg/…). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ≥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the hexa whitelist or the non-hexa heavy classifier (make · cargo · go build · swift build · docker build · pytest · nvidia-smi · train · hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build · aprime_cc · `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan · …) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput) to run on a pool host over ssh — transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only: the load-escalation gate (promote any non-trivial cmd to heavy on high summed non-claude CPU) is absent because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and ships local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`·`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`·`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local — dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`·`~/.hexa-cache`·`~/.pool`·`~/.sidecar`·…) are per-host hidden state, not user-synced, so routing them reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs still route. Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local — they resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them fails (cache absent on the pool host). Trusted surface (every sidecar slash command writes its dispatch via `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` per @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate: within the `/Users/`·`/home/` exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb · direct `hexa.real` · python/python3/py · gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ · `bash`/`sh <script>`) require a fresh `local` sign-off token — the canonical mac fork-storm trigger; USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free — the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 — supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now auto-dispatch candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build · xcodebuild · codesign · …); general-heavy round-robin still excludes macOS hosts so generic build/test fan-out stays off the workstation. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + the command's toolchain actually runnable - `command -v hexa` + `test -x build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help` for hexa, `command -v <compiler>` for C/C++, 0.6.13), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown — never silently falls back to local. Every routed `hexa <verb>` gets `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang` + `HEXA_MODULE_LOADER=$HOME/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_module_loader` (0.18.0 — uniform; pre-0.18.0 only kick/drill/loop/cc got the checkout root, other verbs wrongly got `$PWD`). PATH env passthrough for bare ssh shells. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local — it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool, and its post-`--` remote argv (nvidia-smi · train.log · make) tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux host with no `cloud` subcommand; double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. Worktree→canonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x) previously DENIED; it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` and mirrors that root when it is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir). Runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command. C/C++ compiler routing (0.6.11): the C/C++ compiler drivers (gcc / g++ / clang / clang++ / cc / c++) join the heavy classifier - a C/C++ compile is heavy local compute, same workstation-protection rationale as make/cmake/cargo; they carry no macOS-capability marker, so they round-robin across Linux pool hosts only (the Mac stays the workstation, per @D s12). Absolute-path compiler sign gate (0.6.12): the C/C++ compiler drivers also join the local-bound sign gate within the absolute-path exemption - an absolute-path compile (e.g. /usr/bin/gcc on a /Users path) is the same mac fork-storm trigger as a heavy interpreter, so it now needs a fresh local sign-off token (! sidecar sign local) like hexa/python/sh; relative-path compiles in synced workdirs still route to the pool. First-token basename match keeps it precise. Toolchain preflight + akida exclusion (0.6.13): (a) pi5-akida is excluded from the auto-dispatch candidate set entirely (@D s8 - anima-only; reached only via explicit `pool on pi5-akida`). (b) the host preflight now verifies the command's actual toolchain, not just the workdir - every hexa verb probes command -v hexa + test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp + hexa <verb> --help, and a C/C++ compile probes command -v <compiler>; a host that fails is SKIPPED so round-robin moves on instead of routing-then-failing (the ubu-1 broken-hexa trap). Known limit (@D d6): a binary that passes --help but segfaults at runtime is not caught. mini-equal toggle (0.7.0): `pool mini-equal on` (writes ~/.pool/mini-equal = "on") opts macOS pool hosts (mini) INTO the OS-agnostic general-heavy round-robin as equal workers; is_linux commands still route Linux-only (the toggle only redistributes portable work). Default OFF preserves zero-macOS-offload (@D s12). Not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11) — it opens routing to an idle host, does not disable a guard. Single local-execution gate (0.7.2): a fresh `local` sign token (`! sidecar sign local`, 5min) forces EVERY command local — the one deliberate, user-minted 'run here, do NOT route' path, checked at the top before classification. When the router mis-classifies a local-bound command (diagnostic `ps`/`awk`, a local-fs fix) and ships it to a peer host, the user signs ONCE and routing is suppressed for the window. Subsumes the abs-path-heavy sign gate (signed → top-level returns first; unsigned → that gate still bites). A sanctioned time-boxed user-minted token, not an env-var opt-out (@D s11). Env-prefix + wrapper hardening (0.7.3): `_local_heavy_interp` now strips POSIX env-var prefix (`POOL_DISABLE=1`/`SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE=1`/`FOO=bar`/…, any IDENT=val — parser correctness, not specific-var recognition) AND known leading-position wrappers (`env` · `exec` · `nice` · `timeout` · `command` · `sudo` · `nohup` · `stdbuf` · `ionice`, with per-wrapper option-arg awareness) AND a leading backslash escape (`\hexa`) before reading the real verb — pre-0.7.3 any env-var prefix OR any of those wrappers silently ALLOW'd abs-path heavy invocations, breaking sign-local's single-gate integrity. `eval` quoted-string args remain out of scope (require a real shell parser). Node-package installer pin (0.7.4): npm/pnpm/yarn run LOCAL (cwd-dependent, removed from heavy_words + added to local-bound). Routing-rate baseline counters (0.7.5 — POOL-OFFLOAD m1): every meaningful classifier verdict increments one of 4 counters in ~/.pool/route-counters.tally (routed · local_bound · light_allowed · heavy_failed) so /check reports the % offloaded vs local. Non-classifier exits NOT counted. Race-tolerant. Useless env-prefix advisory (0.7.11 — agent education): SIDECAR_NO_POOL/POOL_DISABLE env-prefix detection at the head of a Bash cmd emits inline additionalContext advisory explaining no-op + pointing to `! sidecar sign local`. Routing unchanged; purely educational so agent stops re-trying silently. big-tree find generalization (0.7.10 — POOL-OFFLOAD m5): heavy_find_subs generalized from single `find $HOME/core/anima` to BOTH `find <HOME>/core/` AND `find ~/core/` (tilde form); routes ANY ~/core/* sibling repo find. Preflight test -d still gates each host. Media tools (0.7.9 — POOL-OFFLOAD m4): heavy_words +2 (ffmpeg/magick) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +4 (ffmpeg/magick/convert/sox · convert/sox English-word substring-collide → first-token-only). JVM compilers + sbt (0.7.8 — POOL-OFFLOAD m3): heavy_words +3 (javac/kotlinc/scalac) + heavy_pairs +4 (java -jar/sbt compile/sbt test/sbt run · pair-only for short names) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +5 (java/javac/kotlinc/scalac/sbt). Node ecosystem (0.7.7 — POOL-OFFLOAD m2): heavy_words +4 (node/deno/bun/ts-node) + abs-path first-token sign-gate adds same +tsx (tsx excluded from heavy_words due to .tsx file-ext collision); mirrors python/hexa fork-storm protection. Heavy hexa verb sweep (0.7.6 — POOL-OFFLOAD m6): heavy_pairs +19 verbs — toolchain (hexa test · hexa bench) · math verifiers (hexa absolute · hexa meta-closure) · math discovery (hexa smash · hexa free · hexa hyperarithmetic) · discovery extras (hexa chain) · 11 drill variants (omega · surge · dream · swarm · reign · molt · wake · forge · canon · debate · revive) — all fork-storm class per the documented hexa 80+ verb catalog. Light verbs (atlas read/lookup · 29 annotation analyzers · 16 HTTP data bridges · lattice verify · gpu lint/disasm) excluded; 3-token verbs (hexa gpu fire · sim-universe · qmirror · atlas register --from-drill) deferred until richer classifier — NOTE: superseded by the 0.8.0 hexa whitelist, which routes ALL of these (except `atlas register`, pinned local in 0.8.1). OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO guard opt-out by design.

plugins.name: limit-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.5 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PostToolUse(Task) hook, implemented in hexa-lang (`_limit_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`) — detects a session/usage-limit signal in a subagent result, AUTO-SNAPSHOTS uncommitted work non-destructively (`git diff HEAD` → `.claude/limit-snapshots/<ts>.patch` + status sidecar; no commit/push/stash/index mutation — shared-worktree safe; recover via `git apply`), then injects a non-blocking checkpoint directive: report progress (committed SHAs vs uncommitted), commit + push uncommitted work, write a `.claude/RESUME.md` resume manifest, stop parallel fan-out and switch to sequential commit-per-unit work. Signal match is case-insensitive (0.1.4). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: question
plugins.description: Quick side-question alias for Claude Code's built-in `/btw`. Two trigger commands: `/question <text>` (primary) and `/q <text>` (2-character alias). Same semantics as /btw — answer briefly (1-3 sentences), then resume the main work. Plugin command (markdown only, no script). Triggers: /question · /q · quick question · 잠깐 · 사이드 질문.

plugins.name: check
plugins.description: In-flight progress dashboard skill, scoped to THIS session ONLY — /check reports ONLY the progress of work currently running in this session: background shell/agent jobs (ps scoped to this session's claude process subtree: pool / verify / ssh / cloud / sub-agent eval'd commands) + their freshly-touched task output files (scoped by CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID; bash output tail + agent-transcript one-liners). The agent pairs this shell scan with TaskList state + active monitors. Read-only; no side effects. Does NOT report other sessions' jobs/outputs, and does NOT survey repo state (no `.log.md` backlog / open-PR / git-status / recent-merge enumeration). Triggers: /check · 상태 · 체크 · 현황 · 진행상황 · status check · dashboard.

plugins.name: lab
plugins.description: 1.14.0 FIXES a catastrophic false-requeue in the lab `tick`/`status` probe (bin/system_harness.hexa): (a) the liveness probe now RESOLVES a job's endpoint from the per-deck `<workdir>/.dft_detach.state` (key=value host·port·identity) when the pods.json job entry lacks an inline host, and (b) adds an UNKNOWN state DISTINCT from GONE — a job whose endpoint could NOT be probed (no endpoint resolved OR no `PROBE` line came back from the host) is UNKNOWN→wait/escalate, NEVER GONE→requeue. GONE now requires a POSITIVE reach (classify() gains a `probed` param: probed && nowork). Live lesson (16 live pods): a campaign keeping endpoints in `.dft_detach.state` (not pods.json) resolved host="" → empty probe → ALL 28 running jobs fell through to GONE→requeue while 16 were ph.x/pw.x-grinding. +6 selftest cases, 25/25 green. /lab — research-lab control-tower (domain-agnostic) with a code-level decision harness (0.7.0) + all-paths autonomy (0.8.0). One persistent mission-control over EVERY in-flight job of a long-running campaign across ANY execution surface (rented pods via cloud · pool hosts · local procs). Ties the manifest (cwd ./pods.json) + event-driven watchers + harvest+verdict + the autonomous harvest→re-dispatch loop into one command. NOT domain-locked — DFT sweeps · ML training fleets · build matrices · render farms · any N-parallel-job campaign with a queued backlog. 1.7.0 makes the ScheduleWakeup HEARTBEAT a first-class BUILT-IN of the autonomous drive: `drive` (and `auto` run sticky cross-turn) now ARMS a self-pacing ScheduleWakeup heartbeat BY DEFAULT — a ~1800s (30min · `--heartbeat <s>` override, clamp 60–3600) liveness-floor tick that re-fires the EXISTING drive body (probe → tick --apply → harvest terminal → resume/triage → re-dispatch queued in parallel → cost check) and reschedules, EVEN WHEN NO WATCHER EVENT FIRES, so the loop NEVER goes idle (catches stalled/silent jobs · a watcher that couldn't attach). It PAIRS with the 1.6.0 event-driven watcher — watcher = the fast path (reacts to events), heartbeat = the liveness floor (timer guarantee of no-idle); same drive body either way, no parallel loop. The heartbeat STOPS rescheduling (drive halts) on the same halts as `auto` — 🏁 DRAINED (all jobs terminal AND queue empty — NEVER ticks into an empty campaign) · 🛑 budget cap (g64) · ⏸ gated:human-only / user interrupt; new `## heartbeat (self-pace · no-idle)` subsection (@H1–H5). bin/system_harness.hexa unchanged. 1.6.0 makes the MONITORING DEFAULT event-driven WATCHERS, not polling: the continuous-monitoring verbs (auto/drive + any keep-watching flow) now DEFAULT to arming the `watch` verb's event-driven watchers (a Monitor/background loop on `hexa cloud tail <host> <log> --until ''` with the failure-first 3-tier exit 0=DONE·3=TIMEOUT-RESUMABLE·4=CRASHED·255=transport-drop) and re-enter on the watcher EVENT, instead of leading with a tick/poll liveness sweep; the tick/poll sweep is DEMOTED to an explicit FALLBACK (used only when a watcher cannot attach — no `hexa cloud tail --until`, a local-only job with no durable remote log, or transport unavailable), retained not deleted; `status` is EXEMPT and stays a one-shot read-only SNAPSHOT (a single on-demand read, not continuous polling); new `## monitoring default — watcher (poll = fallback)` subsection (@W1–W4). bin/system_harness.hexa unchanged. 1.5.0 STRENGTHENS the g59 upstream-reflex to AUTO-DISPATCH the fix UNCONDITIONALLY: a detected upstream-fixable gap (a hexa cloud/deck/CLI limitation · a false-terminal the CLI should have classified · a transport ambiguity · a missing preflight/OOM axis · a cost-leak the rent-guard should prevent · any hand-workaround of the tooling) in watch/harvest/next/auto/drive/pursue or the upstream verb now (a) files the `sidecar handoff add <repo> <gap>` record as before AND (b) IMMEDIATELY spawns ONE background fix agent (general-purpose · run_in_background · isolation:worktree) that fixes-at-source + ships a PR (branch off main · <200 lines · Co-Authored-By trailer · gh pr create · pr-cycle auto-merge · sidecar sync after) — same-turn, no user confirmation (무조건). This is NOT the 4축 auto-decide fork logic — it is an ALWAYS-ON reflex; an upstream code-fix PR is reversible + low-blast-radius so it never falls under the @L3 human-only gates. Brakes = idempotency (no re-dispatch of a gap already in the handoff trail or already carrying an open PR — dedup via the upstream verb trail + gh pr list) + a ≤3 in-flight concurrency cap (queue the rest). Logs one line per dispatch — `🔧 upstream auto-dispatch: <gap one-liner> → agent <id> → PR pending (g59 · 무조건)`; the upstream verb trail additionally shows auto-dispatched fixes (agent id + PR state). 1.4.0 ADDS 4축 auto-decide: at any mid-run LLM decision gate in next/auto/drive/pursue where >1 technically-viable option exists, /lab AUTO-PICKS on the canonical 4 axes (완성도 complete · 단순 simple · 안전 safe · 표준 std — recommend.tape r1, scored 1–5, weighted average default 1:1:1:1, tie→safe wins) and renders one collapsed line `🤖 4축 auto-pick: <option> (완성도=X 단순=Y 안전=Z 표준=W · weighted=<sum>)`, instead of stalling the campaign for the user; the autonomy invariant is preserved — only a human-only gate (credential · irreversible/destructive · genuine open-ended design) and the budget cap (g64) still halt; bin/system_harness.hexa (the deterministic decide() binary) unchanged. Verbs: bare/status · progress (1.1.0 read-only research-ARC bar) · tick [--apply] (0.7.0 deterministic probe→classify→decide harness via bin/system_harness.hexa) · watch · harvest [<id>] · next/redispatch · auto · drive · pursue (0.8.0 — ALL-PATHS autonomy: enumerate EVERY open campaign thread (stalled-resumable jobs · tooling gaps · queued waves · cost-leaks · un-harvested terminals) + fan ALL out in parallel ≤2-3 agents, NO human 'which next?' menu; only genuine human-input gates wait) · mirror [<seed>] (1.2.0 거울방 — DELEGATE to the hexa-loop skill --adapter=atlas, d4 reuse) · stop · cost · queue [add|rm|ls] · upstream [<repo>]. 1.3.0 ADDS an explicit ALL-PARALLEL dispatch principle (↔ demiurge project.tape d_parallel_fire): campaign dispatch fires N candidates across DEDICATED PARALLEL pods (one job→one slot · ranks≤physcores/pod · OMP/MKL/OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1), NEVER bin-packed into a single-pod sequential onstart.sh chain; a stuck single-pod sequential chain (a flagship blocking the rest) is SPLIT onto parallel pods on sight. Threaded into next/auto/pursue/drive + the queue taxonomy (a `queued` candidate fires in parallel on its own slot; a single-pod sequential chain is the anti-pattern to split). Live lesson: a 6-perovskite batch chained on one vast pod → 270-thread/load-119 oversubscription thrash + CaAuH3 blocked 6 other perovskites for days. 1.2.0 ADDS `lab mirror` (the mirror room — pure delegation to the hexa-loop skill's --adapter=atlas preset, the old mirror-loop mining→kick→atlas self-evolution ouroboros, NO reimplementation) + documents a planned lab-lifecycle subcommand set (notebook = append-only journal/decision log · bench = micro-experiment sweep wrapping /micro-exp · review = verdict-matrix audit) as stubs. 1.1.0 ADDED `lab progress` — a read-only campaign-ARC 10-cell bar aggregating verdicts-closed (exports/*/ledger.json + .verdicts/), papers-shipped (PAPER.tape), atlas atom-growth (hexa atlas stats), domain milestones (DOMAINS.tape); each source degrades gracefully (absent → dropped from the mean, not 0%). DISTINCT from `status` (per-job operational live dashboard) — `progress` is the epistemic research-program arc. 1.0.0 RENAMED the skill `system → lab` (research-lab metaphor); the `/system` invocation + every prior trigger (관제탑 · mission control · control tower · campaign status) stay live as DEPRECATED aliases — lossless. All prior verbs preserved verbatim; bin/system_harness.hexa unchanged. Abstract job model. Reuses ./pods.json (cloud dispatch SSOT). Triggers: /lab · 랩 · 연구실 · research lab · lab progress · 연구 진척도 · lab mirror · 거울방 · /system (deprecated) · 관제탑 (deprecated) · 캠페인 현황 · mission control (deprecated) · campaign status (deprecated) · 후보 자율 · 모든 경로 진행 · pursue · tick · 결과보고 추가발사 · 자율 재발사 루프 · control tower (deprecated).

plugins.name: master
plugins.description: /master — owner-only (creator) sidecar maintenance control-plane (master-tier · ~/.sidecar/master marker). Verbs: bare/status (health dashboard) · update (CHECK external Opus/Claude-Code changelogs + internal drift/sync/PR + regenerate the root TOOLKIT.jsonl capability catalog via bin/_gen_toolkit.hexa per s17 → PLAN ranked update candidates → DISPATCH top candidate to `/sbs auto`, running to the `go` checkpoint) · check (read-only availability) · audit (governance↔enforcement integrity · s7) · lint (plugin standards · s1/s3/s6/tier) · sync. Never edits governance SSOT or ships directly — delegates to /sbs. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: draft
plugins.description: Ephemeral scratchpad scaffolder — `/draft <slug>` creates `drafts/<slug>.md` for temporary working notes designed to be discarded. The `drafts/` dir is auto-added to .gitignore so files never commit by accident. Verbs — bare/`list` = enumerate (mtime-sorted) · `<slug>` = scaffold · `add <slug> <content...>` = append a timestamped bullet (auto-scaffolds if missing) · `rm <slug>` = delete one (no confirm) · `clean` = list `rm` candidates (read-only). LLM AUTO-REGISTRATION (0.2.0) — SKILL.md instructs the calling LLM to recognize natural-language signals ("이거 등록해줘"/"메모"/"적어둬"/"register this"/"jot down") and proactively run `/draft add <slug> <distilled content>` without an explicit command, picking a slug by first checking existing drafts (reuse if related) else deriving kebab-case from the subject. Symmetric deletion triggers ("삭제"/"지워"/"delete that note") map to `/draft rm <slug>`. `/draft` files are explicitly throwaway — for cross-repo handoffs that should persist, use `sidecar handoff`. Slug accepts alnum / dash / underscore / dot only.

plugins.name: matrix
plugins.description: Axis cross-product coverage tracker — `/matrix` manages a combinatorial coverage matrix (axis × axis hypothesis/experiment grid) via a cwd-local `MATRIX.tape` SSOT, the same declarative pattern as DOMAINS.tape. Two modes: SQUARE (one axis set via `axes <a…>`; cells = upper-triangle + diagonal pairs, total N·(N+1)/2) and RECTANGULAR (separate `rows <a…>` × `cols <a…>`, e.g. BIO mechanisms × anima axes, total R·C). Verbs — bare/`show` renders the full grid with ✓/· when small (rows≤14 ∧ cols≤14, or square N≤12) else per-row coverage bars ▓▓▓░░, always printing coverage % + next 5 unfilled cells · `done <i> <j>` marks a cell (explicit toggle like /domain milestones; square pairs auto-sorted to a canonical key so order doesn't matter) · `undone <i> <j>` · `next [N]` lists next N unfilled (default 10) · `help`. Drives a systematic combinatorial sweep row-by-row without tracking coverage in prose; SSOT survives across sessions; cells are explicit (not auto-detected) so it's workflow-agnostic. Triggers: /matrix · 매트릭스 · matrix 만들어 · 교차곱 매트릭스 · cross-product matrix · coverage 추적 · 다음 셀 · 어디 채울 차례.

plugins.name: trail
plugins.description: Main-flow return stack — `/trail` is a LIFO deviation stack that records where you were on the MAIN FLOW (the active goal) when you pivot into ANY side-task — an intra-repo tangent/sub-fix OR a cross-repo/upstream fix — so the parent goal is never stranded. Deviations are NOT only cross-repo; a same-repo tangent off the active milestone strands the main flow just as easily. The store is HOME-global (`~/.sidecar/trail.tape`) — NOT cwd-local like `/draft` — so one stack tracks the current dive across intra-repo deviations AND survives `cd` when a detour does cross repos (e.g. anima DECODER → hexa-lang flame → codegen fix). Verbs — `push <return-target>` appends a frame (UTC timestamp + git-toplevel repo basename + return-target text) · `pop` removes the top frame and prints the next frame to resume (or 'back on MAIN FLOW' when empty) · bare/`show` renders the ladder with the deepest frame `★ NOW` at top and the root at the bottom · `clear` wipes · `help`. LLM AUTO-USE (commons g74 companion) — SKILL.md instructs the calling LLM to run `/trail push` the instant work pivots into any side-task (intra-repo tangent/sub-fix or cross-repo/upstream fix) and `/trail pop` the instant it closes, no explicit command, plus `/trail` (show) on long yak-shaves to re-surface the climb-back path. Distinct from `/draft` (cwd-local throwaway) and `/domain` (in-repo milestone SSOT). Portable POSIX script (no GNU-only flags) so it runs identically on macOS + Linux pool hosts. Triggers: /trail · 트레일 · 복귀 스택 · 이탈 기록 · 메인 흐름 표시 · breadcrumb · return stack · 어디까지 했더라 · 원래 뭐 하던 · 곁가지 · detour · climb back · 본 흐름 복귀.

plugins.name: end
plugins.description: Session closure safety check — /end prints a single-shot dashboard of dangling residue in the current repo (uncommitted · unpushed · stash · open PRs by `@me` · marketplace.json ↔ plugin.json version drift · merged-but-undeleted local branches · linked git worktrees) with per-item ✓/⚠/○ marks + recommended actions, and a closing ✅ `100% closure — safe to end` verdict only when every line is green. Read-only — no commit / push / branch delete / worktree remove. Pairs with /ship (the actor) — /end is the verifier. Triggers: /end · 끝 · 마무리 · 종료 · 클로저 · close out · wrap up · 100% closure.

plugins.name: all-bg-go
plugins.description: Parallel fan-out trigger — 'all bg go' → plan table + one background Agent per branch in the same message. REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered. For a self-generating repeatable loop, use /cycle instead. Skill (natural language) + /all-bg-go command + /abg 3-char alias (0.4.2).

plugins.name: all-fg-go
plugins.description: Foreground-sequential fan-out trigger — 'all fg go' → plan table + run the prior turn's branches ONE AT A TIME in the foreground (await each, `▶ i/N → ✅/⚠/❌`, halt on failure). REACTIVE single fan-out of what the PRIOR turn offered — the sequential sibling of all-bg-go (parallel background). No parallelism, no background fan-out; pick it when each branch must be watched in order. For a self-generating repeatable foreground loop, use /cycle-fg. Skill (natural language) + /all-fg-go command + /afg 3-char alias.

plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver scoped to the session's ACTIVE DOMAIN (commons @D g58). The WHOLE family now auto-continues to DEPLETION: each round self-feeds from open milestones (else auto-seeds from `## deferred`), self-continues via ScheduleWakeup, and terminates only when open milestones = 0 AND deferred empty AND no other signal — so every command digs the declared backlog to the bottom on its own (per-round cap throttles WIDTH, auto-continue provides DEPTH; user can interrupt any round). The commands: /cycle — read the active <NAME>.md snapshot, enumerate its open `- [ ]` milestones (off-domain work is forbidden; if no active domain, prompt for `/domain set <NAME>` first); if the next-list is empty, auto-seed ≤N (default 3) candidate milestones — PRIMARY signal = the domain's OWN `## deferred` section (promote the next batch into the milestone board + drain it from deferred so the domain self-feeds its declared backlog), then user hint · prior-turn /gap shortlist · log-tail open thread — via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate (no-signal case = zero milestones + empty deferred + no signal stops with steer-options rather than fabricating); → dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: `sidecar handoff` registry entry status + merged PR + git log) → parallel-plan table → fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) → auto-continue to depletion (depletion-driving by default, not single-round-then-stop). /cycle-full — one-time phase-0 depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS within the active domain, then the same precheck → plan-table → fan-out → auto-continue tail (phase 0 is one-time per goal; subsequent rounds use plain /cycle semantics, self-feeding from deferred, draining to depletion). /cycle-fg-loop — /cycle-fg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous FOREGROUND-SEQUENTIAL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-fg (each round runs inline one-at-a-time, ▶ i/N → ✅/⚠/❌, no Agent fan-out, halt on failure); same depletion termination. /cycle-bg-loop — /cycle-bg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous BACKGROUND-PARALLEL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-bg (each round fans out one background Agent per item, resource-serialized); same depletion termination. (Bare /cycle reaches the same end-state inline, dispatching fg/bg per the sticky ~/.sidecar/cycle-mode marker — the -loop commands are the explicit-pacing surface that names the execution mode.) /cycle-full-loop — /cycle-full once (one-time brainstorm + first fan-out) then handed to /loop /cycle, draining to the same depletion condition. /cycle-all (0.8.2) — like /cycle but with TWO overrides: NO per-round cap (enumerate the FULL open-milestone set + promote the ENTIRE `## deferred` backlog in one round, the `cap>8→confirm` guardrail waived) and NO recommend/select gate (do NOT print a 'recommended priority' shortlist or a 'which to dispatch?' hand-back — fan out EVERY PROCEED row immediately). For 'run everything' intent (전부 진행) instead of a curated ≤N batch. /cycle-fg + /cycle-bg (0.9.1) — STICKY EXECUTION-MODE TOGGLES. `/cycle-fg` sets the session marker `~/.sidecar/cycle-mode=fg` and runs the round FOREGROUND SEQUENTIAL (Stages 0-3 unchanged; Stage 4 executes each PROCEED row INLINE one-at-a-time with visible `▶ i/N → ✅/⚠/❌` progress · NO Agent fan-out · NO parallelism · halt on failure · sbs-like bar). `/cycle-bg` sets the marker `=bg` and runs the round in the default BACKGROUND mode (parallel Agent fan-out + auto-continue to depletion). The marker is STICKY: after `/cycle-fg`, subsequent bare `/cycle` rounds ALSO run foreground until `/cycle-bg` flips back (and vice-versa). Bare `/cycle` reads the marker at its Stage -1 (default `bg` when absent) and dispatches to the matching execution mode. fg = debugging · careful review · cwd-shared / single-threaded-resource work; bg (default) = independent parallel work-bursts that need isolation. Both keep ALL guardrails (SSOT-freshness · dup-race SKIP · leak-sweep · resource-serialization). All safety/correctness guardrails are KEPT (SSOT-freshness, dup-race SKIP, leak-sweep, resource-contention serialization — fanning out everything still serializes EXCLUSIVE GPU resources, never parallel-fires every Agent — throttle-resilience, auto-continue to depletion). All commands share the 5-stage structure + plan-table + dup-race precheck + leak guardrails; they differ only in entry/pacing/cap/execution-mode shape. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). 0.8.0 fixes the per-round shape + adds THE LINTER (@D round_lint): every loop round ends with a result-report (status glyph + PR# + honest verify tier + 7-element easy + progress bar) → debt/split tracking → a `round-lint: N/10 ✓` self-audit of the 10-point contract → loop tail; plus @D oversized_split (≥2 consecutive throttle deaths on one milestone ⇒ split into independently-landable sub-slices, never re-fire whole) + @D handoff_debt_ledger (cross-repo handoff goes to the host-local `sidecar handoff` registry — `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>`, no target-tree write, so no uncommitted-edit debt). cycle-fg-loop/cycle-bg-loop/cycle-full-loop carry the fixed round shape. NL triggers: /cycle · /cycle-full · /cycle-fg-loop · /cycle-bg-loop · /cycle-full-loop · /cycle-all · /cycle-fg · /cycle-bg · 사이클 · 계속 진행 · 다음 라운드 · keep cycling · march on · 전부 진행 · 추천 말고 전부 · 사이클 포그라운드 · 사이클 순차 · 사이클 직접 · foreground cycle · sequential cycle · inline cycle · 한 단계씩 사이클 · 사이클 백그라운드 · 사이클 병렬 · background cycle · parallel cycle · bg 모드로 · 사이클 포그라운드 루프 · fg loop · 연속 순차 사이클 · 사이클 백그라운드 루프 · bg loop · 연속 병렬 사이클. 0.9.2 cross-links the /cycle-bg vs /micro-exp build-vs-fire decision tree (domain-agnostic; mirrored in skills/micro-exp/SKILL.md @D micro_exp_handoff): /cycle-bg is the canonical BUILD lane for candidate orchestration (build+fire integrated per worktree agent), /micro-exp is the FIRE-only lane (pre-built infra assumed; /micro-exp's Stage 1.5 auto-halts back to /cycle-bg on any missing prereq). Tree (kind-abstract): NO matrix → /kick · matrix + ALL infra ready → /micro-exp · matrix + missing infra → /cycle-bg <domain> then /micro-exp.

plugins.name: pool
plugins.description: /pool <args> — sidecar-OWNED `pool` CLI (host roster + remote exec). Source relocated into the plugin (bin/pool.hexa); install.hexa builds ~/.hx/bin/pool.bin + installs the shim. Canonical entry `sidecar pool <args>`; bare `pool` is a kept-working deprecation shim. Verbs: list · add <host> · on <host> <cmd> · status · health · route · bg · refresh · init · rm · clean · desc. Roster SSOT ~/.pool/pool.json (pool-route hook untouched). NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g9.

plugins.name: patrol
plugins.description: 0.1.0 (2026-06-05) /patrol — 자동 순찰 health-monitor: a host-agnostic registry of self-driving jobs + a recurring health patrol that relaunches dead jobs and reports growth. Generalizes the manual fleet auto-patrol (per job: `ps -eo args|grep -c [m]atch` local or `sidecar pool on <host>`; count 0 → run the stored relaunch cmd verbatim; collect a growth line; print a per-job dashboard) into a reusable command. Verbs — add · rm · list · once (one pass) · start (session-scoped recurrence: model re-fires `patrol once` ~1500s via ScheduleWakeup) · start --cron [interval] (PERSISTENT recurrence: model calls CronCreate running `patrol once` — survives session death) · stop (disarms BOTH: clears the session flag + CronDelete the routine) · bare = status dashboard. Registry SSOT ~/.sidecar/patrol/jobs.json (array of {name, host, match, relaunch, growth?}; host=local|pool-host). Relaunch ONLY when count==0; resume-safety is the job command's own responsibility; never touches unregistered jobs. Impl = bin/_patrol.sh (POSIX sh + python3 for JSON round-trip). Triggers — 순찰 · patrol · 자동 순찰 · auto-patrol.

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: 0.4.1 (2026-06-02) DEPRECATION ALIAS — /cloud is now an alias of /pod (the consolidated pod plugin). Use `/pod <subverb>`; /cloud still dispatches for one deprecation version with a banner. The cloud/pod GUARD hooks now ship inside the `pod` plugin (cloud-guard·pod-monitor·monitor-guard·throttle-guard·pods-route bundled there), so gate coverage is unbroken. ── /cloud <args> — wraps `hexa cloud` (runpod / vast.ai dispatch · canonical subcommand form, structured argv — never raw runpodctl/vastai/ssh/scp/curl per commons g8, hard-enforced by the cloud-guard hook). Subverbs: preflight · run · nohup · fire · poll · tail · copy-to · copy-from · **pods · dispatch**. preflight = closed-form GPU mem-budget check (refuses out-of-budget specs before pod spinup · no LLM · $0). tail = live remote-log stream → Monitor bridge (ssh tail -F | sed -u · terminal-exit on a crash-aware default · commons g57). **`pods`/`dispatch` (new in hexa-lang PR #1699)** = per-project active pod/job work manifest at cwd's `./pods.json` (one file per repo — demiurge/anima/hexa-lang each carry their own dispatch state); `cloud dispatch [tree|active|add|verdict|rm]` manages it update-form. Distinct from `cloud orphans`/`reconcile` (which scan GLOBAL `~/.hexa-cloud/pods.jsonl` billing/orphan registry auto-tracked by `cloud run`/`nohup`) — `pods`/`dispatch` is the operator's manual work view per project. Atomic write + .bak rotation + auto `last_updated_utc` stamp (s11). NL trigger via skill. Pairs with commons g8 + g12 + g57.

plugins.name: pod
plugins.description:
0.6.0 (2026-06-04) pod-attribution OWNERSHIP-SPLIT recorder — sidecar OWNS the attribution/label harness (the writer). `pod/bin/_pod_record.hexa` consumes the hexa-cloud `rent-result` facts line (boundary contract: `rent-result pod= host= provider=

state= [project=

] [deck=]`) + caller flags and records {project · candidate · lane · kind} into the cwd ./pods.json work-manifest SSOT with NO OMISSION (underivable field → explicit `unattributed` sentinel + logged WARNING, never silent blank). Fallback chain: explicit flag > derived (project=cwd git-repo basename · candidate=deck ph.in `prefix=` else deck dirname · kind=dft-run/rent · lane=project). Counterpart to the hexa-lang PR making `hexa cloud` POD-OPS-ONLY (it EMITS the facts, is WRITE-FORBIDDEN to attribution). selftest pod/test/record_run.sh 18/18. ── 0.5.1 (2026-06-03) cloud-guard prose-collision fix — detector (a) now scans per-segment via a quote-aware segmenter that SKIPS echo/printf argument spans, killing the `vast instances` English-words false-positive (an echo label tokenized identically to a live `vast instances` CLI call); fail-closed, wrapper (`sudo vastai`)/separator/inline (`bash -c`) detection unchanged, verified 8/8. ── 0.5.0 (2026-06-02) CONSOLIDATION — folds skills/cloud (/cloud · was 0.4.0) + hooks/cloud-guard (0.4.2) + hooks/pod-monitor (0.1.6) + hooks/monitor-guard (0.1.6) + hooks/throttle-guard (0.1.1) + hooks/pods-route (0.1.1) into ONE command-surface plugin: command `/pod ` (thin router over `hexa cloud` · any other cloud subverb passes through · `monitor` → `hexa cloud pods`) + a bundled hooks.json carrying all FIVE guards with their matchers + exit semantics preserved VERBATIM. ── cloud-guard (PreToolUse Bash + Write|Edit|MultiEdit, exit 2 = deny via permissionDecision JSON): hard-block DIRECT rented-GPU provider use — (a) `runpodctl`/`vastai`/`vast` + verb scan, (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp to `*.runpod.io`/`proxy.runpod.net`/`*.vast.ai`, (c) curl/wget to provider APIs, (d) dispatcher-script content scan, (e) inline provider-SDK probe, (f) M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` write-guard (reads pass via `_is_glued_redirect`); ALL provider access routes through `hexa cloud` (commons g8) — 0.5.1: (a) scans per-segment, skipping echo/printf prose. ── pod-monitor (PreToolUse Bash, advisory): non-blocking g57 reminder on `hexa cloud nohup`/`fire`/`run` — SAVE_POD=1 · Monitor on the log · walltime headroom · `./pods.json` register (ghost-pod class). ── monitor-guard (PreToolUse Bash, advisory): g10 while-monitor pattern for general background launches (detach + log + Monitor-on-log); general-local sister of pod-monitor. ── throttle-guard (PostToolUse Task|Agent, advisory): cross-session transient-throttle exponential-backoff coordinator (~/.sidecar/throttle.json), distinct from the usage-cap limit-guard. ── pods-route (PreToolUse Bash, updatedInput): transparently appends `--register` to `nohup`/`fire` so the launched pod auto-lands in cwd `./pods.json` (idempotent; only nohup/fire rewritten). ── /pod = wraps `hexa cloud` (rent · rm/down · list · resolve · run · exec · nohup · fire · poll · tail · copy-to/from · preflight · dispatch · reap · reconcile · watchdog · …) — structured argv, never raw runpodctl/vastai/ssh/scp/curl. fire/nohup emit a `__MONITOR_HANDLE__=` JSON one-liner (grep → attach Monitor to tail_cmd); tail = live remote-log stream → Monitor bridge (commons g57). Implemented in hexa-lang (each guard a self-contained `*.hexa` invoked via `hexa run`). Portable paths only. NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11). KEEPT SEPARATE — pool / pool-route (persistent hosts, NOT cloud pods) are out of scope and untouched.

plugins.name: hf
plugins.description: 0.3.0 (2026-06-02) CONSOLIDATION — folds skills/hf (/hf upload wrapper · was 0.1.0) + hooks/hf-recover-guard (the teardown guard · was 0.2.0) into ONE command-surface plugin: command `/hf <upload|register|mark|verify|status>` (router · the old flag form `--upload`/`--dry-run`/`--selftest`/… still dispatches to `upload`) + a bundled hooks.json (the recovery guard) + bin/ (upload path via stdlib + `hf_recover.hexa` marker CLI). ── ADVISORY rewrite (@L3): hf-recover-guard NO LONGER blocks. PreToolUse(Bash), on a TRAINING-tenant pod teardown (`hexa cloud {down|terminate|stop|rm|remove|destroy} <id>` · `runpodctl {stop|remove} pod <id>` · `vast destroy <id>`; tenant resolved from the M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` `project` field — anima/train/dojo/clm/kosmos/ckpt; non-training / unknown / unreadable → silent PASS) it NEVER exits 2 / NEVER blocks: a missing-or-unverified marker → an HF-registration REMINDER (the runbook, reworded from REFUSING to REMINDER … teardown ALLOWED) on stderr + exit 0; a verified marker (`$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/hf-recover/<podid>.done` JSON hf_repo/sha/ts, re-checked against `huggingface.co/api/{models,datasets}/<repo>` 200=exists) → a brief ✓ note + exit 0; the NO_CLM verified-empty fast-path ($0 / never-built / full-FS scan EMPTY · or ssh-dark + $0/never-built) → a NO_CLM note + exit 0. Self-contained hexa (`_hf_recover_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). ── /hf upload (carried from skills/hf 0.1.0): thin wrapper around `hexa-lang stdlib/hf/upload.hexa` — README 검증(5 required H2 + Caveats≥3) + repo naming(<org>/<family>-<version>-<stage>) → `/usr/bin/find` walk + sha256 → `hf` CLI repo create/upload/tag → audit jsonl + ledger ($HOME/.hexa/hf_upload_audit/). 토큰은 file-backed HF_HOME/token (umask 077 · chmod 600) 으로 ps-invisible · 3-tier 해석 (secret get huggingface.token · ~/.cache/huggingface/token · HF_TOKEN env). 성공 게이트 = 실제 commit URL. ── register/mark/verify/status drive the bundled `bin/hf_recover.hexa` marker CLI (mark writes the marker ONLY after confirming the HF repo exists). Portable paths only ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA / $HOME / PATH). NO env/flag opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11).

plugins.name: hexa-help
plugins.description: /hexa-help [verb] — wraps `hexa --help` (no arg, top-level catalog) or `hexa <verb> --help` (verb-specific). Per commons g7. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: hexa
plugins.description: /hexa <verb> [args...] — generic passthrough to the hexa CLI (`hexa $ARGUMENTS`). Covers the 80+ verbs without a dedicated wrapper; /kick /atlas /verify /cloud take the hot paths. Guarded passthrough: dedicated-wrapper verbs (cloud·deck·kick·atlas·verify) are refused + redirected to their dedicated skill so guards/preflight aren't bypassed. Routed to pool by pool-route. NL trigger via skill.

plugins.name: secret
plugins.description: /secret <args> — wraps the `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain-backed credentials, dancinlab/secret 0.4.0, dual-channel sync). Verbs: get · set · rotate · check · delete · list · service · init [icloud|github <url>] · backup [enable <url>|disable|status] · sync · migrate. Two independent sync channels (iCloud Drive primary + optional private GitHub mirror) push the same encrypted blob — master password is the only decryption secret. Auto-push ON by default once `backup enable` runs (opt out via SECRET_BACKUP_AUTO=0). High-value protection on set (BIP39 wordlist-validated · xprv/WIF/64-hex auto-detect → refuse without --allow-mnemonic + stdin/tty). rotate emits sentinel only.

plugins.name: quota
plugins.description: /quota:quota <args> — Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Single-dispatch picker entry; verb routed via $ARGUMENTS. Verbs: list (= status = bare · UNIFIED all-accounts table in chat, 6-col English header: Nickname · Email · Session Used · Session Reset · Weekly Used · Weekly Reset, ▓░ 5-slot bar + humanized time-to-reset; rows sorted by nickname (else email), case-insensitive; the active account is ★ + fetched LIVE (45s cache), every other account shows its last per-account cache, `—` = never fetched) · all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot, in PARALLEL — the easy toggle to 'refresh everything') · add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture credentials into backup store · optional nickname like claude1) · nick <ref> [<nick>] (set or clear a nickname for an existing account; bare nick arg = clear) · switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback on failure) · remove <ref> (rm · delete · del — drop registry row + backup creds, leaves live ~/.claude.json untouched) · refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens via refreshToken) · autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path — registers the active account iff not yet in the registry; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin so a fresh `claude /login` self-registers next session) · help. The view also auto-registers the live account if untracked (autoadd parity). <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) · email · numeric index — lookup order index→nick→email. Live fetch via OAuth usage endpoint (api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage) + auto refresh-token grant (platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token); 45s per-account cache. Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota/creds/<email>.json (0600 + atomic write). own-accounts-serial only — never a multi-tenant router (ToS red-line). Agent SDK credit (dual-bucket since 2026-06-15) is documented as future direction; current release tracks subscription 5h/7d only. The bare/list view renders from cache instantly then spawns a detached background stale-while-revalidate refresh of stale non-active accounts (cache > 300s, with backup creds) so the NEXT view trends fresh without an explicit `all` — the view never blocks on the network; a dead/expired backup token is skipped for a 1800s cooldown (shows `—` until `refresh <ref>` renews it); the active account keeps a 45s inline live refresh. Both `all` and the background cycle fetch their account set in parallel (`_parallel_refresh` — token-prep sequential, usage GETs concurrent). ⚠ uses non-public Anthropic OAuth endpoints — may break if rotated.

plugins.name: quota-autoadd
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. SessionStart hook — silently auto-registers the active Claude account into the quota registry when a fresh `claude /login` introduces an account not yet tracked. Invokes the quota skill's `_quota.hexa autoadd` (idempotent · always exit 0 · prints one line only when a NEW account lands; already-registered sessions are fully silent). The add path also captures the OAuth blob into the backup store so `/quota:quota switch` works for the new account immediately. Pairs with the `quota` skill — quota-autoadd is the default-on registration layer (manual `/quota:quota add` remains). Resolves the quota binary via $HOME/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/quota/<version>/bin/_quota.hexa (portable; $HOME-based; picks the latest cached version by semver `sort -V`, not mtime). NO opt-out by design (commons g11).

plugins.name: kick
plugins.description: /kick <natural-language seed> — runs `hexa kick --seed "<seed>"` (hexa-lang gap-breakthrough / discovery engine, aliased to `hexa drill`). All args join into the seed. NL trigger via skill (`kick this`, `돌파해줘`, `discover for`). Pairs with commons g6.

plugins.name: verify
plugins.description: /verify <args> — runs `hexa verify "$@"` (cross-project tier rubric, TECS-L-aligned). Forms: <id> (atlas atom), --expr <fn> <n> <v> (numerical), --fence "<claim>" (honest ⚪), rubric / list. Returns 🔵🟢🟡🟠🔴⚪ tier badge VERBATIM. NL trigger via skill (`verify this`, `확인해`, `검증해`, `맞아?`). Pairs with commons g5.

plugins.name: atlas
plugins.description:
/atlas — wraps `hexa atlas` (atlas SSOT surface). SSOT = `compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa` (binary-builtin atlas, ~16088 nodes), served at runtime by static_atlas via TEXT-parse of embedded.gen.hexa. Read verbs: hash · stats [--audit [--scope=rodata|overlay|merged] [--format=json]] · lookup [K] / lookup --prefix=

(K ∈ P|C|L|E|F|R|S|X|Q) · dump [K] [--json]. Write verb (ONE): register --from-verify (or 2-op) · register --from-drill --seed "" [--rounds N] [--engine mk9|mk10] — runs the verb in-process and on 🔵/🟢 folds the verified AtlasNode DIRECTLY into compiler/atlas/embedded.gen.hexa, visible to the next lookup with NO rebuild, NO staging shard, NO intermediate file (commit embedded.gen.hexa with normal git to share). Export verb (NEW): export [--out PATH] — writes the live atlas out as a portable .n6 file (default n6/atlas.n6, an export artifact only, NOT the runtime SSOT). NL trigger via skill (`atlas lookup`, `atlas stats`, `아틀라스 조회`, `아틀라스 등록`, `register from drill`, `atlas absorb`).

plugins.name: domain
plugins.description: 0.12.0 (2026-06-07) AUTO-USE half restored — the retired `skillopt-hook`'s SessionStart auto-inject now lives INSIDE the domain plugin: `hooks/hooks.json` fires `bin/_domain_skill_inject.sh` at every SessionStart, injecting the activated skill (`~/.sidecar/domain-skill/active-skill.md`, written by `/domain activate`) as additionalContext so a trained skill is auto-applied with NO command (closes the 0.11.0 gap where `activate` wrote a file nothing read). New `/domain agent-active on|off` toggles the optional `~/.sidecar/domain-skill/agent-active` nudge marker (one-line 'PROPOSE /domain train for repeatable scored tasks — never auto-run'). OPT-IN + safe: the hook is silent when nothing is activated, NEVER trains, always exits 0 (fail-open). This restores full parity with the two retired plugins (TRAIN + USE) entirely under `/domain`. 0.11.0 (2026-06-07) ML subverbs unify the retired `skillopt` + `skillopt-hook` plugins under `/domain`: `/domain doctor` (skill-train readiness) · `/domain train [--bg]` (NATIVE pip-free SkillOpt loop — rollout → reflect → edit → held-out gate via `claude -p` = your Claude Code subscription, NOT a metered API; only a SEPARATE skill.md is optimized — the domain's own DOMAIN.md / verdicts / log are NEVER touched) · `consume <skill.md>` · `activate`/`deactivate` (write ~/.sidecar/domain-skill/active-skill.md) · `status`/`log`. Bundled `examples/toyqa` (6 format-sensitive QA items) proves the loop end-to-end with NO external data/pip. ML verbs route to `bin/_domain_ml.sh` (+ `bin/_domain_ml.py` engine); ALL existing domain verbs route to `_domain.hexa` UNCHANGED. 0.10.1 (2026-06-03) `_is_name` now accepts a DIGIT-LEADING NAME — first char may be an UPPERCASE letter OR a digit 0-9, so `/domain init 8VERB` works (previously rejected: digit-leading start); subsequent chars unchanged (UPPERCASE/digit/`-`/`+`). 0.10.0 — bare `/domain` + `/domain list` now LINT for scattered report-like `.md` (a basename token audit|report|verdict|gap|finding|impl OR an ISO date like `2026-06-02`) sitting loose at the repo root or under `docs/` WHILE a domain is active, and emit a `⚠ 분리보관 감지` advisory naming each stray with its `/domain absorb <file>` runbook (excludes the domain's own pair / CHANGELOG / README / `.log.md` / already-absorbed pointer files; read-only). 0.9.0 — new `absorb <file> [--state]` verb enforces combined-storage (합산보관) over scattered docs/*.md: folds an existing separate report/finding `.md` into the ACTIVE domain's single doc pair (default → <NAME>.log.md as a dated `## <ISO> — absorbed <rel>` entry embedding the body; `--state` → <NAME>.md snapshot), then replaces the source with a one-line `<!-- absorbed into … -->` pointer (idempotent — an already-absorbed pointer file is skipped; refuses to absorb the domain's own pair). Resolves the active domain via the same _get_active / _snap_path / _log_path logic the other verbs use. Maintain UPPERCASE <NAME>.md (snapshot = final-goal milestone checkboxes) + sister <NAME>.log.md (append-only step log) at project root — auto-scaffolds both, defaults NAME to uppercase basename of git root. Verbs: /domain <NAME> (SELECT the session's active domain → later verbs default to it + show a final-goal progress bar `▓▓▓░░ NN% · done/total` from the snapshot's checkboxes) · /domain (show active) · /domain list (alias ls — repo-wide index; with a DOMAINS.tape roster it is AUTHORITATIVE: tables each registered domain ★ = active · @goal · progress · location, progress/@goal stay DERIVED so the checked-in roster holds only NAME→path and never churns; flags ghosts + unregistered; no roster → legacy disk-scan) · /domain list --sync (reconcile DOMAINS.tape with disk — bootstraps the roster) · /domain <task> (append `- [x]` to log) · /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) · /domain done <match> (flip [ ]→[x]) · /domain title <text> (alias subtitle — OPTIONAL `@title:` display header: icon · name · alias, e.g. `🧠 IIT4 — 의식 측정자(尺)`, rendered in place of plain `active domain: NAME`; absence keeps current output, no lint warning) · /domain new <header>. Progress is final-goal-based (snapshot completion), not log-based — it doesn't drift as the log grows. Path resolution: a DOMAINS.tape roster row (`@domain NAME := "path"`) wins FIRST, so a domain can live at ANY path (e.g. `domains/RUNTIME/RUNTIME.md`); absent a roster entry it falls back to root → folder-nested → root-default-scaffold (log follows the snapshot's dir), so folder-nested meta-domains no longer regenerate an empty root scaffold each call. The folder-nested fallback only matches when the nested file's first line is the domain header `# <NAME>` (guards against false-matching an unrelated same-named file like a digest on case-insensitive filesystems). 0.8.8: /domain set now SELF-HEALS the DOMAINS.tape roster (auto-registers an on-disk-but-unregistered domain — the gap that ghosted anima's LIFE) and warns on a stale SSOT shadow (the working-tree <NAME>.md is UNTRACKED or BEHIND origin/main → progress/closure may read a stale local copy; reference_domain_init_untracked_ssot); a perpetual `@goal` (no-termination marker like `종료 조건 없음`/`perpetual`/`open horizon`) renders a ♾️ perpetual badge so the progress bar reads as frontier-depletion, NOT a 100% completion countdown (per feedback-closure-is-physical-limit).

plugins.name: domain-doc-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.0 — PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory enforcing combined-storage (합산보관) over scattered docs/*.md: when a report-like `.md` is written WHILE a domain is active, it nudges the model to fold the file into the active domain's single doc pair (`<DOMAIN>.md` + `<DOMAIN>.log.md`) via `domain absorb`, instead of leaving it scattered as a separate doc. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_domain_doc_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). FIRES (exit 2, non-blocking additionalContext) only when BOTH hold: (1) the written file is a `.md` whose basename or first ~40 content lines match a report heuristic — a token (audit|report|verdict|gap|finding|impl) OR an ISO date like `2026-06-02`; AND (2) a domain is CURRENTLY ACTIVE for this (repo-root × session), read from the same `$HOME/.claude/domain-active.tsv` store `domain set` writes (exact (root,session) row, else the most-recent any-session row for the root). SILENT (exit 0) otherwise — a plain `notes.md`, a non-`.md`, the active domain's own `<DOMAIN>.md` / `*.log.md` / CHANGELOG / README, an already-absorbed pointer file, or when NO domain is active (so it never fires in a fresh repo / a sub-shell with no domain context). De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Pairs with the `domain` plugin's `absorb` verb (the runbook it points at). Advisory-only (per guards-narrow-scope). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: domain-cycle-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.0 — Stop|SubagentStop (cycle-close) enforcement that the ACTIVE domain's doc pair (`<DOMAIN>.md` + `<DOMAIN>.log.md`) is updated EVERY CYCLE, so per-cycle progress is never lost and the domain SSOT never goes stale (매 사이클 도메인 문서 업데이트 강제). Sibling of the advisory `domain-doc-guard` (same family): that one folds a SCATTERED report `.md` INTO the domain; this one catches the inverse — work happened this cycle but the domain doc was NEVER touched. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_domain_cycle_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). At cycle-close: (1) resolve the ACTIVE domain for (repo-root × session) from the same `$HOME/.claude/domain-active.tsv` store `domain set` writes + its snapshot path from `DOMAINS.tape` (both roster forms `@domain NAME := "path"` and `@D NAME := "path" :: domain`); none/unregistered → SILENT no-op. (2) detect work-this-cycle CHEAPLY — `git status --porcelain` (dirty tree) ∪ files newer than a per-(root,session) cycle-start marker; no work → SILENT. (3) if `<DOMAIN>.md` OR `<DOMAIN>.log.md` is among the touched files → SILENT (compliant). (4) work happened BUT domain doc untouched → ENFORCE: DEFAULT advisory-strong `systemMessage` (never hard-traps the user), telling the agent to append a `<DOMAIN>.log.md` step / flip a milestone before closing; HARD-BLOCK opt-in via env `DOMAIN_CYCLE_GUARD_HARD=1` or marker `~/.claude/domain-cycle-guard.hard` emits the harness Stop block decision `{decision:block}` instead. Honors `stop_hook_active` (never loops). The cycle-start marker is refreshed after every close (work / no-work / enforce) so each cycle measures relative to the prior close — one enforce per cycle window. Stop is harness-reachable for plugins (verdict-gate + celebrate already register Stop hooks). Gate is a real precondition (active domain + work + doc untouched), not an opt-out switch.

plugins.name: go
plugins.description: /go [hint] — proceed with the most-recently proposed action / continue the paused flow without further confirmation. Bare "go" single-word user message also catches as natural-language alias. Identical semantics to '그냥 진행해' / 'continue' / 'just run it' — re-read the last agent message, identify what was proposed/paused/awaiting (a plan · a recommended option · the next /sbs step · the next /cycle round · a /cycle-fg halted item · an AskUserQuestion default), and execute it verbatim. Optional `/go <hint>` disambiguates (e.g. `/go ubu-2` selects host, `/go retry` re-fires a failed step, `/go skip` advances past, `/go all` applies to every candidate). NO new plan, NO re-question — stateless continuation token. If no recent proposal exists, ask one short line rather than fabricate. Distinct from /sbs (plan-first sequential) and /cycle (autonomous fan-out): /go is a token, not a runbook. Triggers — '/go', bare 'go' (single-word), '진행', '계속', '다음 진행', 'proceed', 'continue', 'ok go', 'yes go'.

plugins.name: roi
plugins.description: /roi [scope] — enumerate LOSSLESS performance/resource/speed improvement candidates as a ranked TODO list. "Lossless" = no functional regression, no API breakage, no semantic change, no test failures — only HOW the same behavior is achieved more efficiently. Scope: bare /roi = active domain (if set) ELSE cwd repo · /roi <message> restricts to that scope (file path · subdirectory · feature name · layer · language · hot path). Output is a single ranked table `| # | category | item | impact | effort | risk | how | evidence |` where category ∈ {⚡ speed · 🧠 perf (CPU/algo) · 💾 resource (mem/disk/network) · 🔋 efficiency}, sorted by impact-to-effort ratio (highest ROI first), risk=low ONLY (any candidate that could risk behavior change is dropped — that's NOT lossless). Discovery: scope read → anti-pattern grep (N+1, re-parse-per-call, O(n²) where O(n log n) trivially applies, unbounded growth, sync I/O on hot path, sort+take-1 vs min, regex recompile per loop, dict lookup not hoisted, …) → bench-ground claims where measurable (@D bench_kernel_choices, 가정 말고 벤치) → rank. Cap default top 10/category. Drop-count surfaced when items excluded for risk≥medium. Triggers — '/roi', '/roi <scope>', 'roi 뽑아', 'roi list', '무손실 개선 할일', '성능 개선 할일', '자원 개선 할일', '속도 개선 할일', '효율 개선 할일', 'lossless improvements', 'perf wins'.

plugins.name: gap
plugins.description: /gap — multi-axis gap exploration. 42 breakthrough-strategy lenses · 8 families (F4 + F6 each have 6 lenses; `occams-razor` lives in both — hypothesis side and design side). Bare /gap = inline-triage + deep-dive only hot families. /gap full = exhaustive 8-subagent fan-out. /gap <scope> targets the sweep. /gap list prints the catalogue. Surfaces + prioritises gaps; never fixes.

plugins.name: sidecar
plugins.description: 0.11.0 — `sync` realign GUARD: the realign (`git checkout main` + `reset --hard origin/main`) targets ONLY the hx-managed marketplace clone (`~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/sidecar`) and runs ONLY when the tree carries nothing to lose. A pure precondition gate `_sync_realign_decision()` REALIGNs only when the repo IS the marketplace clone (a user dev checkout at a different path, or carrying a `.sidecar-dev` marker, is SKIPPED), the tree is clean, AND HEAD is on `main` or a non-main branch with NO unpushed commits ahead of origin/main (an ahead branch holds in-flight work → SKIP). Concurrent `sidecar sync` callers serialize on a short mkdir lock. No env opt-out (s11). New `sidecar sync-guard-selftest` proves the 4 cases on throwaway repos (clean-on-main → REALIGN · clean-on-feature-ahead → SKIP · dirty → SKIP · dev-checkout → SKIP). Protects a shared dev checkout from being yanked to main by the post-ship `sidecar sync` / auto-sync hook; the `fuse` launchd daemon is a separate circuit, untouched. 0.10.0 — `fuse` FREEZE-IMMINENT tier (@L4 · mini kernel-panic 2026-06-02): a new MEM_HARD_PCT (default 92 · above the mem_pct admission line) tier SIGKILLs the single largest non-claude memory consumer INCLUDING active hexa runs — this tier ALONE drops the 'never touch active hexa runs' exemption (that exemption is what let a 76GB runaway hexa_run starve WindowServer into a watchdog panic) while STILL never touching claude (s9) or sshd. New fuse_top_mem_hexa() selector + mem_hard_pct config key; `fuse selftest` extended to prove the hexa_run reap spares sshd/claude with zero freeze risk. /sidecar <args> — thin wrapper over the `sidecar` marketplace CLI (host-local, on PATH via `hx install sidecar`). 0.9.0 — `fuse` mem-reap: a MEM trip now SIGTERM-REAPS the top-RSS (memory) consumer to actually RECLAIM RAM (renice only lowers CPU priority — frees no pages — so a mem-trip renice could not stop a memory freeze; that was the bug behind a 5GB balloon → mem 99% → sshd starved → SSH banner never sent). cpu/load trips keep the reversible renice. The reap target excludes claude (s9), sshd (NEVER reaped — keeping SSH alive is the point), and self/hexa/awk/ps; the mem reclaim is mandatory, no opt-out (s11). New `fuse selftest` proves the reap on a ulimit-capped synthetic sleeper hog while sparing sshd/claude-named processes, with zero freeze risk. 0.8.1 — `mirror` now SKIPS fully-orphaned/superseded plugins: it picks the highest NON-orphaned cache version (every version carrying `.orphaned_at` → plugin deprecated → not command-mirrored), so a renamed plugin's stale command no longer double-surfaces in the TUI alongside its successor (e.g. the `system`→`lab` rename: orphaned `system` was still emitting `~/.claude/commands/system.md`). Generic — no plugin name hard-coded. 0.8.0 — NEW `handoff` verb: the cross-project handoff registry (~/.sidecar/handoff/handoff.jsonl, single SSOT for cross-repo gap/patch/bug/RFC handoffs per commons g11·g36·g48·g59·g60; retires the per-repo INBOX domain). `handoff add <repo> <text...>` appends an open JSONL entry · `handoff ls [<repo>]` tables entries (filter by repo) · `handoff done <id>` flips status → done · bare `handoff` prints a counts summary. JSONL not markdown (structured carrier · project.tape s2); store is repo-external runtime state, portable via $HOME; atomic tmp+rename writes. 0.7.0 — `reap` verb (reap stale `hexa-lsp` idle > 2h; dry-run by default, `reap --kill` terminates; never touches claude @D s9 or active hexa runs) + `paths add/rm` SIGN-GATED (key `paths`: `! sidecar sign paths` first, since the whitelist overrides pool-route). Verbs — init (scaffold project.tape + CLAUDE.md symlink + LATTICE_POLICY.md) · sync (pull marketplace + refresh cache + installed_plugins.json + enable plugins + mirror commands) · mirror (refresh ~/.claude/commands/ mirrors so plugin commands are bare-invocable — `Skill("imagine")` instead of `Skill("imagine:imagine")` — auto-runs at sync tail) · shadow [plan] (drop the FORCED `plugin:command` namespace #15882 — mirror+disable every command/skill plugin, splicing SKILL.md triggers into the bare command so it stays lossless; hook/mcp kept; `plan` = dry-run; re-asserted at sync tail) · unshadow (revert) · paths (0.6.0 NEW — manage the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST `~/.sidecar/local-paths`; pool-route 0.10.0 forces any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed token starting with a registered prefix to run LOCAL. `paths` lists, `paths add` registers cwd, `paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `paths rm <prefix>` removes a line by exact match. Seeded on first use with `~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/`. Atomic writes; whitelist itself IS the opt-in — no env opt-out — @D s11 safe.) · sign (user sign-off gate). `sign <key>` mints a 5-min token at ~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign unlocking agent edits to a sign-gated governance SSOT file — the sign-guard hook hard-denies those edits until a fresh token exists; keys are `commons` (commons.tape), `project` (project.tape), `gitignore` (.gitignore), and `paths` (~/.sidecar/local-paths). Bare `sign` / `sign --list` shows active tokens; `sign clear [<key>]` removes them. Multi-user enable profiles — `profile [<name>]` shows/sets the enable profile (minimal = core only · hexa = core + hexa-lang · full = everything, default · master = full + creator-only). `master [on|off|status]` mints/removes the ~/.sidecar/master marker. `enable`/`disable <plugin>` force one plugin on/off (per-plugin override), `reset <plugin>` drops the override; each re-applies the install. State in ~/.sidecar/profile + plugin-overrides.json; install.hexa enables per .claude-plugin/profiles.json tiers — plugin disable, not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11 safe). Args pass through unchanged.

plugins.name: watch
plugins.description: /watch <video-url-or-path> [question] — give Claude the ability to watch a video. Downloads with yt-dlp (YouTube · TikTok · X · Instagram · Vimeo · local file), extracts auto-scaled frames with ffmpeg (2 fps / 100-frame hard cap, duration-aware budget; --start/--end for a denser focused window), pulls a timestamped transcript native-captions-first, then hands frames + transcript to Claude so it answers grounded in what's on screen and in the audio. Whisper API fallback is OPTIONAL — captions-only works keyless and a missing key degrades to frames-only, never hard-fails; the key is read env → sidecar `secret` CLI (macOS Keychain `groq.api_key`/`openai.api_key`) → ~/.config/watch/.env. SessionStart hook surfaces a one-line setup status; setup.py auto-installs ffmpeg/yt-dlp (brew on macOS). Ported to sidecar from bradautomates/claude-video@c333c22 (plugin `watch` v0.1.3, MIT, Bradley Bonanno).

plugins.name: inject
plugins.description: Immediately inject the latest sidecar commons.tape + project.tape into the CURRENT session and sync the local install for the next session. /inject (and `/ij` short alias) runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch) AND prints the current commons.tape + (cwd's) project.tape so the model picks them up this turn. 0.2.1 (2026-05-30) fixes a command-flattening bug — the `!`-command was newline-separated, but when the harness flattens it to one line `set -e` swallowed the rest (`set -e sidecar sync echo …`), breaking the run; rewritten as a single `;`/`&&`-separated line that works flattened, dropping `set -e`.

plugins.name: ship
plugins.description: Atomic ship tail for sidecar plugin changes — /ship -m "<msg>" <path>... stages explicit paths (never -A/-u), credential-scans the staged diff (rpa_ · sk- · hf_ · AKIA), commits, pushes origin/<branch>, then runs `sidecar sync` (marketplace pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). Mechanical tail of @D ship / commons g27; the agent owns version bump + surface lockstep + message FIRST. Bare /ship (no -m / no paths) is helper mode (0.3.2): lists uncommitted changes + emits a ready-to-edit `/ship -m "..." <paths>` template instead of a usage error, exits 0, still never auto-stages. NL trigger via skill (ship · 배포 · 출시).

plugins.name: bypass
plugins.description: Anti-punt — DEFAULT BEHAVIOR (auto-fires, not opt-in). Universal self-check before any move that hands control back to the user (interactive input · unauthorized destructive · external visible · explicit user-review request); if all NO, just execute. Extensible catalog of patterns: 'next user action:' blocks · 'Should I proceed?' confirmations · 'Want me to check?' deferrals · option-trees with obvious default · over-clarification · defer-by-waiting · excessive recap. Cross-project always-on enforcement in commons.tape ≥0.7.3.

plugins.name: brainstorm
plugins.description: Iterative brainstorming — given a seed, generates ideas in rounds and keeps going until depletion (no genuinely new ideas vs prior rounds; hard cap 8 rounds). For breadth over selection. Skill (natural language) + /brainstorm command.

plugins.name: easy
plugins.description: Easy (friendly) response style — 7-element pattern (icon · name · alias · plain-line · analogy · ASCII · compare). Skill (natural-language trigger) + /easy command. 5 language samples: en · ko · ja · zh · ru. /easy command body now points at $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT with a cache-resolved fallback (0.1.2) so the bare-invocable user-level mirror works. 0.1.3 — layperson+ASCII 강화 (easy-auto 0.3.0 미러): 5개 언어 styles 에 ASCII 구조 4종 템플릿 + 일반인 gold(전문용어→생활어) + 번역 체크리스트 + 측정 축 ascii-structure-template-usage 행 (두 styles/ 트리 byte-identical 미러 · additive).

plugins.name: easy-doc
