plugins.description: Easy-doc authoring — turn a technical file (or a topic) into a layperson-friendly sibling `<name>.easy.md`. Applies the canonical easy reference (hooks/easy-auto/styles/easy.<lang>.md, source-of-truth — not duplicated): the 7-element pattern (icon · name · alias · plain-line · analogy · ASCII · compare), the 4 ASCII structure templates (before-after · tree · side-by-side · structure-sketch), and the jargon→everyday translation checklist. Skill (natural-language trigger) + /easy-doc command. Ships one worked sample (samples/cache.easy.md). Authoring-only (markdown output, no executable hook logic); reads the input → applies the easy contract per document shape → writes the sibling `.easy.md`. Pairs with the `easy` skill (response style) — easy-doc is the FILE-output sibling. 0.2.0 — wraps the `hexa easy` builtin (scaffold → LLM slot-fill → lint gate; manual fallback when unsynced). 0.1.0 — initial.

plugins.name: easy-auto
plugins.description: 0.4.0 (2026-06-06) ASCII 강제 강화 (2층위). (1) 주입 헤더 최상단에 front-load 된 ASCII 필수 mandate + 4종 템플릿(전후 →/트리 ├─└─/나란히 │/구조 [ ]──▶) inline skeleton + 1줄 self-check + 제외목록 — reference 본문에 묻혀 살리언스 낮던 ASCII 항목을 응답 직전 가장 먼저 보이게 끌어올림 (기존 body 그대로 아래 · additive). (2) 신규 Stop 이벤트 _easy_ascii_stop.hexa — transcript 마지막 assistant 메시지가 non-trivial(≥400자·코드/JSON 과반 아님)인데 fenced ``` 블록 안에 box-drawing 문자(│├└─┌┐┘→▶╲╱) 없으면 advisory systemMessage 출력. decision:block 절대 미사용(verdict-gate 선례 — 자율루프 trap 방지)·stop_hook_active true면 즉시 skip·미충족 precondition silent skip. sidecar @D s7 충족. 0.3.2 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0. 0.3.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit + PreCompact + PostCompact hook — auto-injects the `easy` (friendly) 7-element response style as additionalContext so the pattern is active from turn 0 without the user calling /easy. Reads ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/styles/easy.<lang>.md (env `EASY_LANG` override, default `ko`); falls back to easy.md (English base) when the lang variant is missing. UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the style never fades; PreCompact + PostCompact survive auto-compaction. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_easy_auto.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Pairs with the `easy` skill (manual /easy trigger remains — easy-auto is the default-on layer). `EASY_LANG` only SELECTS the style language (default `ko`) — not a disable switch. NO opt-out by design (commons @D g11 / sidecar @D s11); 0.1.2 removed the `EASY_LANG=off` suppress path. 0.2.0 — 한국어 substring 트리거 '설명'/'쉽게' 추가 (UserPromptSubmit 시 verb/noun/adverb 모든 어형 catch: '설명해'/'설명해줌'/'설명'/'설명서'/'쉽게'/'쉽게 알려줌' 등; 오탐 가능성 수용) + 발동 시 1줄 banner emit ('🎓 easy 모드 활성 — 7-요소 패턴 적용 …'). 기존 inject는 그대로 always-on (trigger gating 아님 — banner만 추가). 0.3.0 — 일반인+ASCII 강화: 5개 언어 styles/easy.<lang>.md 에 ASCII 구조 4종 템플릿(전후/트리/나란히/구조) + 일반인 gold(전문용어→생활어 BEFORE/AFTER) + 일반인-번역 체크리스트 + 측정 축 ascii-structure-template-usage 행 추가 (additive · 훅 로직 무변경).

plugins.name: easy-paper
plugins.description: /easy-paper <slug-or-paper.md> — authors a layperson `.easy.paper.md` companion of a paper. Reads a PAPER/<slug>/ dir (per PAPER.tape roster) or a given paper .md, then emits `<slug>.easy.paper.md` with FOUR paper-specific sections: (1) 평이-초록 plain-language abstract, (2) ASCII figure sketches (the easy 4-template set: before-after · tree · side-by-side · structure), (3) 비유 섹션 analogy-driven method/finding, (4) "이 논문이 한 일" one-paragraph what-it-did summary — all under the easy 7-element pattern. Source-of-truth = easy-auto/styles/easy.<lang>.md (point + summarize, NOT duplicated). Sidecar to `paper` + `easy`; READ-ONLY on the source paper (never edits main.tex / PAPER.md / PAPER.tape / verdicts; a negative-result paper stays a negative result — no invented numbers). One worked sample bundled (sample-cost-routing.easy.paper.md). 0.2.0 — gates the finished companion through the deterministic `hexa easy lint` builtin (per-axis 측정 축 + PASS/FAIL; manual fallback when unsynced); scaffold not used (paper-specific 4-section output). 0.1.0 — initial.

plugins.name: research
plugins.description: 0.2.5 (2026-06-04) fix: arxiv 429 rate-limit handling — `_arxiv.hexa` now captures the HTTP status (`curl -w "\n%{http_code}"`) and detects a rate-limit (HTTP 429 OR a literal `Rate exceeded.` body) explicitly, entering a backoff-retry loop (sleep 3s · 6s · 12s, honoring arXiv's ~3s min inter-request interval) before reporting failure. On persistent rate-limit it prints `research/arxiv: rate-limited by arXiv (HTTP 429) — retry shortly` and exits non-zero, instead of the previous misleading `no results` (which masked transient 429s as empty feeds). `no results` is now reserved for a genuine HTTP-200 empty feed; the success parse path is unchanged. Research-fetch tools — `/research:arxiv <query|id>` searches the official arXiv API or fetches by id (title · authors · abstract · pdf); `/research:yt <url-or-id>` extracts YouTube caption transcript via the InnerTube ANDROID client. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_arxiv.hexa` / `_yt.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`); HTTP via curl, no API key. Error paths (network · parse · no-captions · bad-id) exit non-zero so callers can tell failure from a real result.

plugins.name: gh-stack
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) GROWS TEETH (guard-teeth-sweep @L5 · s7) — adds a DETERMINISTIC per-layer line-count gate `bin/gh_stack_lint.sh <layer-below> [head]`: sums `git diff --numstat <base>..HEAD` (added+deleted) and FAILs (exit 1) over the fixed 200-line cap, run before each `gh pr create --base <layer-below>`. Graceful skip (exit 0) when git absent / diff uncomputable; the 200 cap is fixed (no env opt-out · s11). Stacked-PR workflow skill — proposes `gh stack` commands (enabled repos) or the manual `gh pr create --base previous-layer` fallback. Encodes the <200-lines-per-layer · 1-concern governance.

plugins.name: paper
plugins.description: /paper <args> — arxiv-style LaTeX paper scaffolder (v0.12 — s3 portable-path fix: the help text for `verify-block` now shows `$HOME/.hx/bin/hexa` instead of a hardcoded `/Users/ghost/...` absolute, matching the `env_var("HOME")` idiom the runnable already uses). Verbs: list [--sync] · set <slug> · show [<slug>] · goal <text> · title <text> · ms <text> · done <match> · todo <text> · new <slug> · sample [<name>] [<dest>] · samples · monograph-init <slug> [N] · fill <letter> · fig <size> <prompt> <out.png> · compile [dir] · lint [dir] · companion {init|sync} · outline [dir] · pipeline ... · atoms <domain> · verify-block ... · bib add <id> · pr-roll <OWNER/REPO> <since> · arxiv-prep [<dir>] · help. v0.11 fixes (INBOX #112 from demiurge ANTIMATTER): (1) pr-roll — early reject for bare repo name + jq `\\#` escape so LaTeX `\#` survives; (2) atoms — LC_ALL=C around BSD awk + lowercase compare + matches `fn <name>(` dispatch lines; (3) arxiv-prep — resolves dir to absolute path before tar subshell. v0.10 PAPER.tape roster + per-paper PAPER.md/log.md unchanged.

plugins.name: imagine
plugins.description: /imagine <prompt-file> <out.png> [-s size] [-b backend] [-m model] — generic AI image generator. Two backends out of the box: `fal` (queue+poll fal.ai, default — `secret get fal.api_key`, model = openai/gpt-image-2 firm-pinned default) and `openai` (sync /v1/images/generations — `secret get openai.api_key`, model = gpt-image-1). Canonical fal-style size tokens (`square_hd` / `landscape_16_9` / `portrait_16_9` / `square`) translate per-backend. Prompt always read from a file (provenance), payload JSON via mktemp (prompt never on argv). Backend pluggable — drop a new `_backends/<name>.hexa` to add one.

plugins.name: output-trim
plugins.description: 0.1.5 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) stdout trimmer — when a simple single Bash command would emit >8000 chars, transparently rewrite via `updatedInput` (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED, else Claude Code ignores the rewrite and stdout is never trimmed; the 0.1.3 fix) to pipe stdout through a minimal hexa-native kernel: consecutive-line dedup ([×N] counter) + per-line truncation to 200 chars. Small outputs <8000 chars pass verbatim. Skipped on any shell metacharacter so compound pipelines / redirects / heredocs / background jobs / command substitutions and other PreToolUse(Bash) rewrite hooks compose without conflict. Exit code preserved via `set -o pipefail`. Kernel chosen by benchmark on 120 real Bash-stdout samples ≥8000 chars — Pareto-dominates the TF-IDF salience + MinHash near-dup paragraph dedup approach of the original `wilson-output-trim`. 0.1.4 — dead retired-rule source citation in plugin.json replaced with the current no-opt-out rule (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: subagent-route
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. [POC] PreToolUse(Task|Agent) observation hook + SessionStart observation-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_subagent_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). v0.1.0 is observation-only — it does NOT deny, NOT rewrite. On every Task/Agent fire it dumps `tool_input` (key list + agent_type + prompt preview + isolation) to `~/.sidecar/subagent-route.log.jsonl` (capped 100 lines, jsonl format mirroring pool-route's route-log) and emits a non-blocking `additionalContext` advisory suggesting the equivalent `ssh <host> -- claude --print --no-tty -p '<prompt>'` rewrite. SessionStart event reads the last 5 observations back as additionalContext so the model sees recent subagent fires without recon. PoC purpose — probe the actual tool_input payload shape at runtime (Claude Code docs leave Agent/Task tool_input fields under-specified vs Bash), and probe the `additionalContext` UX before a real-rewrite v0.2.0. Always exits 0; advisory only.

plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: 0.2.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and considers a worktree only when its branch is in that merged set OR its HEAD commit truly landed on origin/main. 0.2.0 adds FOUR unconditional live-work guards so an active worktree is NEVER wiped mid-task: (a) dirty-tree (uncommitted changes → SKIP), (b) recent-mtime (HEAD commit or any file modified < 1h → SKIP), (c) cwd-in-use (a live process' cwd is in the worktree, via lsof → SKIP), (d) HEAD-ancestor real-merged check (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <wt-HEAD> origin/main`) — a vanished `origin/<branch>` ref alone is IGNORED so a reused branch name can't condemn fresh work. (e) Atomic prune: `git worktree remove` is the sole removal path; on failure NOTHING is deleted (no partial / manual rm). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs → short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-auto-sync
plugins.description: 0.2.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. SessionStart hook that runs `sidecar sync` once per Claude Code session, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_auto_sync.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the gap between an upstream `git push` on origin/main and the local plugin cache — no more manual `sidecar sync` between sessions. Silent on success; brief stderr advisory on failure. Skipped silently only on a real precondition: the `sidecar` binary not on PATH (fresh machine without `hx install sidecar` yet). Adds ~1-2s to session startup (network pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) — no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for load-assessment commands (project.tape @D s9). Fires when a Bash command looks like a Mac load check — `uptime` (word) · `loadavg` / `vm.loadavg` (substring) · `ps -A` adjacency or `ps -Ao pcpu` · `top -l ` (macOS) / `top -b ` (Linux) · `getloadavg`. Emits non-blocking systemMessage reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks — pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) — the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: askq-text
plugins.description: 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion) → plain-text chat redirect, in hexa-lang (`_askq_text.hexa`, via `hexa run`). AskUserQuestion renders an arrow-key option-tree box in the TUI; this host prefers plain CHAT — questions asked as ordinary text, answered conversationally. A PreToolUse hook cannot re-render the box as text (no transform API), so the hook DENIES the call with an instruction telling the model to re-emit the SAME question as plain text in its reply (state it in prose, list any options inline marking a recommendation, accept a free-form answer). A FORM redirect, NOT anti-punt — the model SHOULD still ask, just in chat rather than the box (distinct from the `bypass` skill, which is about NOT asking clearly-OK reversible work). Plan approval keeps ExitPlanMode and is NOT touched. Unconditional — the tool name IS the precondition, NO env-var / flag escape hatch (project.tape @D s11). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: lsp-rebuild
plugins.description: 0.2.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. PostToolUse(Write|Edit) auto-rebuild for hexa-native LSP servers, in hexa-lang (`_lsp_rebuild.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When the agent edits an LSP grammar source matching `*/lsp/<lang>_lsp.hexa`, the prebuilt `~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` is stale (next LSP session would run the old grammar), so the hook rebuilds it in the BACKGROUND — `nohup env HEXA_MAC_BUILD_OK=1 hexa build <src> -o ~/.local/bin/<lang>-lsp-hexa` (detached `&` → PostToolUse returns immediately; output → ~/.sidecar/lsp-rebuild.log) — and emits a non-blocking advisory. Keeps the build artifact in lockstep with the canonical .hexa grammar SSOT so a grammar change auto-propagates without a manual rebuild. Rebuild-not-source-run because the hexa LSP stdio server needs the `read_stdin_n_c` FFI which only links into a COMPILED binary. Fires on the `*/lsp/*_lsp.hexa` pattern AND on hexa-lang's own LSP (`self/lsp.hexa` monolith + `self/lsp/*.hexa` submodules — NOT named `*_lsp.hexa`), rebuilt via its `tool/build_hexa_lsp.hexa` orchestrator → `<repo>/bin/hexa-lsp` (project.tape @D s11). Pairs with the `*-lsp` wiring plugins (hexa-lsp · kosmos-lsp · n6-lsp · hxc-lsp). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: step-by-step
plugins.description: 0.15.0 — Step 1(Parse) RESOLVER-FIRST: `!`-exec's recommend-axes `resolve-mode <args>` (0.5.0) and OBEYS its two-line output (`mode: …[ ← inherited from recommend-default]` + `resolved: mode=.. axis=.. weights=.. source=…`) as authoritative — DETERMINISTIC resolver reads `~/.sidecar/recommend-default` + computes LOCKED precedence (explicit `auto:<axis>`/`<weights>`/`manual` > inherited FIXED axis for bare `auto`/no token > 4-axis 1:1:1:1 > MANUAL) IN CODE, so bare `/sbs auto` reliably inherits a standing FIXED axis (s7 teeth — was 100% model prose). Resolver unsynced/errors/empty → FALL BACK to in-context `# default mode:` directive (advisory · same SSOT). `CLI 파일읽기 ≠ 모델 파일읽기` 화해 1줄 added to both sbs.md + step-by-step.md; existing model-targeted '모델은 파일 읽지 말라' wording preserved. lockstep recommend-axes 0.5.0. /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook with TWO modes (auto · manual default · 0.5.0 2-mode redesign) + 0.6.0 자동 4축 QA after ship (functional·visible·conformance·regression · regression FAIL → auto-revert · 나머지 alert · SKIP=PASS-equivalent · 결과는 plan.md `## qa-results`). MANUAL (the DEFAULT) — chat-form disambiguation: ONE question per round in chat (NOT AskUserQuestion), each round framed with the easy-mode 7-element scaffold (icon · canonical name · friendly alias · plain-line · everyday analogy · ASCII sketch · option compare-table · one-line recommendation) and closed with `→ A · B · 또는 자유응답` (free-form answers explicitly OK). Repeat round-by-round until ambiguity = 0, THEN render a `🎯 합의된 결정셋 (N개)` ASCII tree of every accumulated decision and PAUSE — user types `go` to confirm or `Qn=<other>` to flip ONLY that one (re-render after each flip). On `go`, write `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` (gitignored; frontmatter slug/mode/auto-weights/created + task brief + locked decisions + next-action checklist ending with a ship line + completion criteria) and LAUNCH A BACKGROUND AGENT (general-purpose, run_in_background=true) with a self-contained prompt — the agent runs end-to-end including ship + auto-QA 4축, no further user input needed; user can leave. AUTO — same chat-form scaffold rendered, but each question is AUTO-PICKED by the 4-axis weighted average instead of waiting for the user: 완성도 (complete · robust + edge-case coverage) + 단순 (simple · Occam) + 안전 (safe · blast radius minimized) + 표준 (std · fits the sidecar pattern), default 1:1:1:1, inline override `/sbs auto:safety <task>` (single-axis force) or `/sbs auto:complete=2,simple=3 <task>` (explicit weights). AUTO still pauses at the agreement screen so auto-picks get a user pre-commit checkpoint. Mid-run new ambiguity also opens a chat round + re-renders the agreement screen. Mode = FIRST arg token `auto`[:`<axis-or-weights>`] | `manual` (default manual). `legacy-manual` = the old per-step pause behavior — 1-version deprecation banner, use plain `manual` going forward. `go` is NOT a mode (use `/go` to continue the paused flow). ALL modes halt on a step failure (failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). 0.6.0 auto-QA 4축 (handoff agent ship 직후 자동 실행): functional (새 endpoint/verb 응답) · visible (URL/path/surface 노출) · conformance (locked decision ↔ 코드 1:1) · regression (기존 surface 미손상). Hybrid fail 정책 — regression FAIL만 auto-revert (`git revert <SHA> && git push && sidecar sync`) + banner, 나머지 3축은 ship 유지 + plan.md `## qa-deferred` append + banner. ALL PASS/SKIP → banner 없이 plan.md `## qa-results`에 ✓ 라인만 append. 0.7.0 — ship+QA 직후 자동 HANDOFF.md 9-section 인계 doc (PR 매트릭스 · SSOT 파일 인덱스 · 새 API surface · 컴포넌트 트리 · env 변수 · 다음 우선순위 · 한계+guard · memory 인덱스 · 한 줄 시작 가이드; `memory/project_<slug>_handoff.md` 미러 · plan에 handoff verb 면 end-user dossier bundle 도). 0.8.0 — plan.md frontmatter `status: active` stamp 규약 + `## locked decisions` 불릿 기계가독 contract 확장 `- @L<n> (<axis>): <option> · assert:<grep|file|verdict> <arg>` (grep <pat>·`!pat`=부재 / file <path>·존재 / verdict <slug>/<id>·`.verdicts/` non-empty). 신규 `plan-guard` 플러그인이 이 marker 를 sub-agent 프롬프트에 verbatim 주입 + ship 시 `plan-lint` 검증; `@L` 없는 기존 plan.md 는 skip (후방호환). 0.8.1 — plan 라이프사이클 명시: write 시 frontmatter `status: active` stamp 必 (정확히 1개만 active), closure 시 `status: done`(폐기 시 `abandoned`)로 flip — plan-guard 활성추적(@L6)이 stale plan 의 @L 을 잘못 강제하지 않게 함. 0.9.0 — chat-form 7-element 라운드 scaffold 를 `hexa easy` 빌트인에 배선(easy-doc·easy-paper 와 동일 패턴): `hexa easy scaffold` 가 결정적 7-슬롯 골격 · LLM 이 창의 슬롯만 채움 · `hexa easy lint` 가 라운드 prose 품질의 advisory 게이트(disambiguation/auto-pick 로직은 빌트인 독립 · 항상 실행). SSOT = hooks/easy-auto/styles/easy.<lang>.md (중복 금지) · toolchain 미동기 시 self-check graceful fallback. 0.10.0 — Step 0.9 인계가 **ad-hoc HANDOFF.md 작성을 폐지** (commons @D g36 · g59 · g60 · s16: handoff 는 단일 `sidecar handoff` 레지스트리로, scattered .md / per-repo INBOX 금지). 대체 carrier 둘: (a) intra-repo 9-section dossier → `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` 의 `## handoff` 섹션 append (gitignored · s16), (b) cross-repo follow-up → `sidecar handoff add <repo>` 레지스트리. memory mirror = `MEMORY.md` 인덱스 한 줄 (별도 pointer 파일 폐지). 신규 `handoff-guard` 플러그인이 root/`domains/HANDOFF.md` 작성을 PreToolUse hard-deny → 재발 차단 (s7 — 규칙+enforcement 동반; `inbox/` 는 atlas-submission 등으로 과적재된 이름이라 guard 제외). 0.11.0 — Step 0 parse 가 recommend-axes 기본값을 상속 (명시 mode token 없을 때 `~/.sidecar/recommend-default` 매핑: present→MANUAL · auto→AUTO 1:1:1:1 · complete|simple|safe|std→해당 축 forced; 우선순위 EXPLICIT > recommend-default > MANUAL). 0.12.0 — 상속을 **파일 읽기 → in-context 디렉티브 준수**로 전환 (s7 신뢰성 fix): recommend-axes 훅이 매 턴 주입하는 `# default mode:` 디렉티브가 단일 SSOT — sbs 는 더 이상 `~/.sidecar/recommend-default` 를 별도로 읽지 않고 (모델이 자주 건너뛰던 skippable step 제거) 이미 컨텍스트에 있는 디렉티브를 OBEY: `# default mode: AUTO …`→AUTO 4-axis 1:1:1:1 · `# default mode: FIXED <axis> …`→AUTO 해당 단일 axis forced (auto-proceed · agreement 1회) · 디렉티브 부재(present)→MANUAL. 우선순위 EXPLICIT arg token > in-context 디렉티브 > MANUAL fallback. recommend-axes 0.4.0 의 AUTO/FIXED 디렉티브가 /sbs mode 도 관장한다는 절을 실어 enforcement 동반 (s7). 0.13.0 — disambiguation 보증 강화 (stepwise/ambiguity→0 audit): Step 0.5 re-scan→zero 루프를 advisory → **non-skippable hard gate** 격상 (ambiguity > 0 인 채 agreement 진입 금지 · AUTO short-circuit 불가 · 유일 exit = explicit zero re-scan) · up-front 전체 enumeration MANDATORY 명시 · no-fabrication 보증 MANUAL → **BOTH 모드** 확장 (AUTO 는 enumerate 된 옵션만 pick · missing FACT 는 invent 말고 agreement 에 surface) · Step 0.6 agreement 에 zero-gate precondition 추가. sbs.md alias 도 요약 수준 동일 보증 (parity). doc-only (NO hexa). 0.14.0 — BARE `auto` 토큰(정확히 `auto`, `:axis`/`:weights` 없음)이 standing FIXED recommend-default axis 를 상속 (예전엔 항상 4-axis 1:1:1:1): in-context `# default mode: FIXED <axis>` 디렉티브가 있으면 그 단일 axis forced(나머지 셋 weight 0)로 AUTO · `← inherited from recommend-default` 노트 (no-token 상속과 동일 wording) · 디렉티브 AUTO/present/absent 면 4-axis 1:1:1:1 유지. 명시 `auto:<axis>`/`auto:<weights>` 는 inherited FIXED axis 보다 우선 (axis 우선순위: explicit auto:<axis> > inherited FIXED axis (bare auto / no token) > 4-axis 1:1:1:1). step-by-step.md + sbs.md mode-parse prose lockstep · MANUAL/agreement/handoff 불변 · doc-only (NO hexa).

plugins.name: deck
plugins.description: /deck <domain> <slug> '<spec-json>' — 🍞 도메인 input deck 빵틀 generator. Thin wrapper around `hexa-lang stdlib/deck/gen.hexa`. v0.2 — s3 portable-path fix: the Step-1 runnable now invokes `hexa run $HOME/.hx/install/hexa-lang/stdlib/deck/gen.hexa …` instead of a hardcoded `/Users/ghost/...` absolute (pool-route 0.15.0 ships the companion `stdlib/deck/gen.hexa` LOCAL pin so the now-`/Users/`-free command still stays local). RTSC QE el-ph first impl (4 files emit: vc-relax.in · scf.in · ph.in · run.sh; spec keys = anchor · press · q_grid · sscha? · soc?; PSL 1.0.0 pseudo auto-branch on `soc` flag). Output dir = exports/<domain>/decks/<slug>/. chem/chip/bio/nuclear/material stub (TODO — first RTSC impl confirms pattern, then graduation = add emit_<domain>() in stdlib/deck/gen.hexa + manifest stub→full flip). Generic dispatch (d4) — domain routing by manifest, no per-domain hardcoded class. Verbs — bare/list (registered domains roster) · spec <slug> (re-emit audit) · <domain> <slug> '<spec-json>' (emit). Post-emit report = 4-file size + closed-form preflight (`hexa cloud preflight --kind dft-phonon --atoms N --nq M`) + d16 governance hint (pool ubu-1 free 1-iter dry-run before cost-bearing rent · catches QE directive/basis/pseudo errors free). RTSC emitter detail: vc-relax.in (ibrav=0 + CELL_PARAMETERS @ press kbar), scf.in (ecutwfc 80 · ecutrho 800 · methfessel-paxton smearing), ph.in (q_grid · alpha_mix 0.3 · el_ph_sigma 0.005 · electron_phonon='simple'), run.sh (cd $(dirname $0) + source ~/miniforge3 conda activate qe — detached env loss 방지 per demiurge MEMORY 'Detached remote env loss'). sscha=true → append minimize.in + sscha_loop.sh anharmonic driver. soc=true → swap UPF to .rel-pbe-spnl-rrkjus_psl.1.0.0.UPF (full-relativistic). Demonstrated by demiurge RTSC 2026-05-29 — CaAuH3_SOC + 9 anchor sweep (h3o · h3f · h3p · h3cl · AcBeH8 · YH10×3 press) decks emit. Triggers — '/deck', '빵틀', 'deck-gen', 'input deck 생성', 'bake deck', '빵 굽기', 'deck 만들어', 'cooking deck'.

plugins.name: dojo
plugins.description: /dojo <domain> <slug> '<spec-json>' [--lang=hexa|py|both] — 🥋 cloud training-job 빵틀 generator. Thin wrapper around `hexa-lang stdlib/dojo/cli.hexa` (invoked via `hexa run $HOME/.hx/install/hexa-lang/stdlib/dojo/cli.hexa …` — stdlib hot-swap, no binary rebuild; `dojo` is ALSO registered as a first-class `hexa dojo` verb in self/main.hexa loop/stdlib-pattern dispatch for the next toolchain build). deck(DFT input deck)의 학습 형제 — spec-json 받아서 cloud pod에서 돌릴 training-job 한 세트를 emit: job.hexa (.hexa 드라이버 · `hexa cloud nohup <host> <log> -- python3 train.py` 배경 디스패치 + poll/tail hint) + train.py (.py 페이로드 · HuggingFace AutoModelForCausalLM + Trainer fine-tune + perplexity eval) + run.sh (.sh 글루 · cd $(dirname $0) + d16 free `pool on <host>` import dry-run hint + hexa run job.hexa). Output dir = exports/<domain>/dojo/<slug>/. --lang=hexa|py|both (default both) filters the emitted subset — hexa→job.hexa only, py→train.py only, both→all 3. llm 도메인 full impl (d6 first); vision/rl/tabular registered-but-stub (honest exit-1 + graduation TODO = add emit_<domain>() + dispatch branch in stdlib/dojo/gen.hexa). Generic dispatch (d4) — domain routing by manifest, no per-domain hardcoded class. WHY hexa-native 무수정: .py/.sh 는 hexa 제너레이터의 자기 런타임 write_text 가 써내는 emit-string 산출물 — agent Write/Edit/Bash-redirect 아님 → hexa-native PreToolUse 가드 범위 밖 (deck run.sh emit 과 동일 원리). spec keys (llm) = model(gpt2) · dataset(wikitext) · dataset_config · epochs(1) · batch_size(4) · lr(5e-5) · max_steps · host(ubu-1) · output_dir(out). Verbs — bare/domains (도메인 roster full/stub) · <domain> <slug> '<spec-json>' [--lang] (emit). Triggers — '/dojo', '학습 빵틀', 'dojo-gen', 'training job 생성', 'bake training job', 'cloud 학습 굽기', 'dojo 만들어', 'train job 빵틀'.

plugins.name: hexa-loop
plugins.description: /hexa-loop [seed] [--adapter=atlas|bench|byte-diff|smoke] [--diverge-only] [--engine mk9|mk10] [--max-rounds N] — canonical HEXA-LOOP, the universal self-evolving loop. ONE loop drains ANY active domain (math · perf · codegen · web) to genuine depletion via a PLUGGABLE verify-adapter. Each round = (1) DISCOVER — mining-style lens divergence + connect convergence saturate (the old /mining auto, now internal) PLUS a `hexa kick`/`hexa drill` breakthrough probe on the ranked top-3 candidates (the old mirror-loop 'mining→kick→' step, now internal), (2) 🔌 VERIFY-ADAPTER auto-selected by active-domain kind — atlas (math: `hexa atlas register --from-drill` / `hexa verify`, fold to embedded.gen.hexa) · bench (perf: measure → byte-diff IDENTICAL + roofline % (achieved/binding-roof, binding = compute/memory roof auto-selected by arithmetic-intensity vs ridge-point; denominator = achieved-peak microbench, NOT spec-sheet) + Δ vs baseline) · byte-diff (codegen: regen byte-equality) · smoke (web/service: liveness assert), (3) ABSORB the verified result into the domain SSOT — atlas embed · *.bench.md · .verdicts/<slug>/<id>.txt · ledger, (4) feed the verified result as the next-frontier seed, (5) explicit disk checkpoint per round (throttle-resilient · mining 0.5.0 discipline), (6) depletion test — 0 new leaf AND 0 new edge AND 0 new absorb AND all lenses @depleted = real-drained → terminate; else ScheduleWakeup 1200s long interval (cache window). Guards inherited VERBATIM from mirror-loop — HONEST g63 (drill/verify SKIP/FALSIFIED/🟠/🟡/⚪ rejected, never absorbed; g5 paste-verbatim), IDEMPOTENT (adapter-appropriate dedup pre-check skips an already-absorbed result), ACTIVE-DOMAIN ONLY (g58 · reads ~/.sidecar/active-domain · refuse off-domain). Preset shorthands — `--adapter=atlas` ≈ the old /mirror-loop (math atlas-fold loop) · `--diverge-only` ≈ the old /mining (discover stage only, no verify/absorb). Adapter EXTENSION hook — drop `~/.sidecar/loop-adapter/<name>.md` (verify-step + absorb-target schema) to add a custom adapter, mirroring mining's `~/.sidecar/lens/<name>.md`. ABSORBS + RETIRES /mining (now the discover sub-stage / --diverge-only) and /mirror-loop (now the --adapter=atlas preset). The /kick command is KEPT — it is the `hexa kick`/`hexa drill` CLI-verb wrapper that this discover stage CALLS, not an orchestrator. Defaults — adapter=auto · engine=mk9 · max-rounds=0 (NO cap · run to genuine depletion, the default; explicit N>0 = optional backstop) · budget=0 (local). Triggers — '/hexa-loop', '헥사 루프', '범용 루프', '자가진화 루프', 'loop any domain', 'universal loop', 'self-evolving loop', '거울방', 'ouroboros 드라이버', 'mining→verify→absorb 회전', 'self-evolve atlas', 'lens 발산', '점잇기', 'diverge only', 'pluggable verify loop'.

plugins.name: micro-exp
plugins.description: /micro-exp [scope] — context-driven micro-experiment sweep orchestrator (domain-agnostic). Self-enumerate sweep candidates from the conversation / active domain (no manifest file — like /cycle) → Stage 1.5 per-candidate infra existence check (HALT to /cycle-bg when missing) → rent pods (≤budget) → copy inputs → run → Monitor (kind-appropriate alive-check) → on JOB DONE harvest+down + dispatch parse Agent → atlas register on green for closed-form-atom-shaped kinds (skip for observation-only) → aggregate ledger at exports/sweep/<batch>/. Inverse of one-big-run: N small verify-able experiments in parallel; honest sweep (FALSIFIED is CLOSED-negative, never skipped — g63). 0.4.0 lands the domain-agnostic generalization: kind is ABSTRACT (materials wall · LLM bench · web smoke · build bench are all examples among many; no kind hard-coded as default), Stage 1.5 infra existence check (verify the four generic prereqs runnable+inputs+parser+workdir per candidate; HALT with N/M candidates missing dispatch infrastructure — /cycle-bg <active-domain> required first when any miss), and a /cycle-bg ↔ /micro-exp decision tree cross-linked in both SKILL.md (BUILD lane vs FIRE lane; mirrors skills/cycle/SKILL.md). Local-pool adapter generalized — works on ANY kind that has a runnable + parser. Bare = current context · arg = scope filter. Distinct from /cycle (milestone-driven loop) and /all-bg-go (reactive single fan-out). NL trigger via skill (sweep these, verify-driven sweep, build then fire, infra missing).

plugins.name: stdlib-ssot-guard
plugins.description: 0.2.0 (2026-06-06) exact `pub fn NAME` collision PROMOTED to a HARD DENY: a non-stdlib `.hexa` Write/Edit introducing a `pub fn NAME` whose NAME already exists as a `pub fn` in hexa-lang stdlib (grep-exact) now `permissionDecision:"deny"` + redirect (import · rename · `/stdlib promote`). Only an EXACT public-name collision is gated — target guaranteed non-stdlib (stdlib SSOT itself skipped) so no self-match false-positive; private `fn` collision + abs-import nudge stay ADVISORY. PreToolUse(Write|Edit) + SessionStart for commons @D g61 (hexa-lang stdlib = SSOT for shared code, primitives AND domain engines), in hexa-lang (invoked via `hexa run`). Also nudges (non-blocking): (a) abs-path import of another repo's `.hexa` — promote to stdlib + import "stdlib/…"; (b) `fn NAME` already a stdlib `pub fn` — import it (best-effort grep, fail-safe). SessionStart checks the single physical stdlib root (~/core/hexa-lang/stdlib). Edits under hexa-lang/stdlib skipped. Pairs with the /stdlib skill. NO opt-out by design (s11).

plugins.name: stdlib
plugins.description: /stdlib — hexa-lang stdlib SSOT helper (commons @D g61). `check` (bare) scans the cwd repo for g61 violations: abs-path cross-repo `.hexa` imports + local fn names duplicating a hexa-lang stdlib `pub fn`. `promote <file>` prints the move-to-stdlib + thin-shim runbook (engine⊥adapter; the phi_native / IIT4 #1051 flow). Read-only scan; promote prints a runbook. Pairs with the stdlib-ssot-guard hook.

plugins.name: walkie
plugins.description: 📻 /walkie — session-to-session walkie-talkie over a UNIX domain socket, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). Two Claude Code TUI sessions exchange one-line JSON messages: the CALL side is a CLI one-liner (`socat - UNIX-CONNECT:<peer>.sock`, fallback `nc -U <path>`); the receive side is the agent arming a persistent Monitor — `on` directly on `socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork -`, OR, when the `walkie-arm` hook's capture daemon owns the socket and durably captures to `<self>.inbox.log`, on `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` (phase-2 coexistence — `on` auto-retargets) — so each received line surfaces as a chat task-notification. Local mailbox + roster under `~/.sidecar/walkie/` (roster.json maps handle -> socket path + sid + ts) so pool-route keeps it LOCAL. Radio verbs — `on` (listen: upsert self + print the Monitor command, retargeting to the inbox log when walkie-arm is present) · `call <handle> <text...>` (direct; roster-miss falls back to the default `<handle>.sock` path so a peer that armed via `arm` — bind-only, unregistered — is still reachable, graceful peer-offline hint when not live) · `all-call <text...>` (every peer) · `scan` (roster table, alive marked via `test -S`, dead rows flagged not deleted) · `handle <name>` · `arm [<handle>]` (print the exact persistent-Monitor listen command for self or a named peer so a parent can paste it into a sub-agent prompt — main->sub steer) · bare `/walkie` (status). OLD verbs (listen/send/broadcast/who/nick) still dispatch as silent aliases. NL triggers via the skill — '무전' · '워키토키' · '세션끼리 통신' · '옆 세션에 보내' · 'call session' · 'socket message' · 'inter-session' · 'steer sub-agent'.

plugins.name: walkie-arm
plugins.description: 0.2.1 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.2.0 (2026-06-02) phase-4 SUB-AGENT AUTO-ARM-ON-SPAWN — a new PreToolUse(Task|Agent) `inject` verb makes EVERY spawned sub-agent steerable mid-flight over walkie WITHOUT the operator pasting `/walkie arm` output into the spawn prompt. A sub-agent is NOT a session, so the SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit hooks never fire for it; this verb (plan-guard inject pattern — additionalContext only, never `updatedInput`, never a deny) mints a UNIQUE child handle per spawn (`sub-<frag>-<seq>`; frag from the Task subagent_type/description sanitized to [a-z0-9-], seq from a persisted `~/.sidecar/walkie/seq` counter), registers it into roster.json via `_register_self` (so the main session sees it via `/walkie scan`), spawns the SAME detached capture daemon for the child socket (steers sent between the sub's polls are durably captured to `<child>.inbox.log`), and emits an additionalContext block telling the sub it is handle `<child>` + the canonical Monitor-arm / offset-drain listen command + how to ack via `/walkie call <parent>`. Idempotent — skips (empty output) when the spawn prompt already contains the `📻 walkie auto-arm` marker (re-spawn / nested). s11 precondition-gated (self handle set + socat|nc present), else silent exit 0 — NO opt-out switch. 📻 SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook — phase-2 AUTO-RECEIVE for the `walkie` plugin, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). A UNIX socket is a connection not a queue, so phase-1 `/walkie on` drops every message that arrives while no one is bound. walkie-arm closes the gap: on SessionStart it upserts self into `~/.sidecar/walkie/roster.json` and spawns a DETACHED capture daemon (`socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork OPEN:<self>.inbox.log,append,create`, fallback `nc -lkU`; setsid→nohup) that OWNS the socket and appends every received line to `<self>.inbox.log` — idempotent via a `<self>.daemon.pid` pidfile + pgrep guard, stale socket `rm -f` before bind. On SessionStart + every UserPromptSubmit it drains `<self>.inbox.log` from the byte offset in `<self>.offset`, emits new lines as additionalContext (`📨 from X: ...`, capped ~20 with an overflow summary), and advances the offset (resets to 0 on truncation/rotation). Self handle from `~/.sidecar/walkie/self` (shared SSOT with the `walkie` command plugin). hexa-lang (`_walkie_arm.hexa`). Pairs with `walkie` 0.3.0 whose `on` retargets to `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` when the daemon owns the socket.

plugins.name: walkie-mcp
plugins.description: 0.1.4 (2026-06-02) broker no longer gates on a walkie handle. plugin.json declares the http MCP url (127.0.0.1:7717) UNCONDITIONALLY so the host always tries to connect — but launcher AND server both `exit 0` when no `~/.sidecar/walkie/self` handle was set (the common case), leaving `/mcp` RED (connection refused). Fix: the broker now LISTENS regardless of the handle (gate is node-present only, s11) and the inbox tail resolves the self handle LAZILY per 1s tick — a handle set AFTER startup activates idle-push with NO restart. Verified: with no handle, `initialize` over http succeeds; then setting a handle + writing an inbox line emits the channel notification live. 0.1.3 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. 📻 walkie phase-3 IDLE-PUSH — an HTTP/SSE-transport MCP channels server (sidecar @D s15 1.8 allows HTTP/SSE MCP; STDIO MCP stays forbidden). walkie(0.3.0) + walkie-arm(0.1.1) capture inbound session-to-session lines into `~/.sidecar/walkie/<self>.inbox.log`, but an idle session only drains them on its NEXT turn (poll). This plugin closes that gap: a single local Node broker (Streamable HTTP on 127.0.0.1:7717) tails `<self>.inbox.log` from a dedicated `<self>.mcp.offset` (separate from walkie-arm's `.offset` so the two never contend) and, per new JSON line `{from,text,ts}`, emits an MCP channel notification `method:notifications/claude/channel` `params:{content:"📨 from <handle>: <text>"}` — which Claude Code PUSHES into the live-but-idle session (no turn needed). The server declares the `experimental["claude/channel"]` capability the host requires; plugin.json exposes it as a `channels` entry + an http url-based `mcpServers` entry (NOT stdio command-based → lint passes). receive-PUSH only — send stays the existing `/walkie call` socket path (no re-implementation). SessionStart fires a detached daemon-launcher (`_walkie_mcp_up.hexa`; pidfile + pgrep guard, walkie-arm pattern; on first run `npm install --omit=dev @modelcontextprotocol/sdk` into the plugin dir if node_modules absent — node_modules never committed). sidecar's FIRST non-hexa (Node) executable. IDLE-PUSH requires Claude Code launched with `--dangerously-load-development-channels walkie-mcp` (or `--channels walkie-mcp` once GA) AND a first-party provider (Bedrock/Vertex skip) — experimental research-preview. NO opt-out switch (s11) — gates only on the real precondition node-present (NOT the handle), else graceful exit 0. NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only). 0.1.2 — STATEFUL multi-session transport fix: per-session Server+transport keyed by the SDK session id (was a single shared Server re-connect()ed per request, which orphaned a prior client's long-lived push SSE stream and surfaced host-side as 'socket connection closed unexpectedly' mid-turn); channel emits now fan out to EVERY connected session.

plugins.name: walkie-hexa
plugins.description: 0.1.2 (2026-06-03) inject-only hook wrapper now ALWAYS exits 0 — a transient toolchain rebuild (hexa.real returning compile-error rc=2 mid-`sidecar sync`) can no longer block prompt submission for this advisory/inject hook (it never denies — only emits additionalContext); genuine guards keep their exit-2 deny. 0.1.1 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. 📡 SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook — the EMIT/sender side of the walkie family for hexa-lang releases (NO MCP, sidecar @D s15). Watches `$HOME/core/hexa-lang` git HEAD: on every SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit it reads `git -C $HOME/core/hexa-lang rev-parse --short HEAD` and, when the sha DIFFERS from the last-broadcast one in `~/.sidecar/walkie/hexa-lang.last-sha`, auto-broadcasts a one-line `📡 hexa-lang <short-sha>: <commit subject>` to EVERY live peer via the walkie all-call roster loop (verbatim from `/walkie all-call` — resolve each handle's sock from `~/.sidecar/walkie/roster.json`, build a `{from,text,ts}` JSON line, send via `socat - UNIX-CONNECT:<sock>` else `nc -U <sock>`, skip self, skip offline peers). Receiving is ALREADY handled by walkie-arm (capture daemon) + walkie-mcp (idle-push) — this plugin ONLY emits, never re-implements receive. Per-sha atomic `mkdir ~/.sidecar/walkie/.hexa-lang-lock-<sha>` lock → exactly ONE session fires per new sha even under concurrent ticks (the loser just records the sha). FIRST-SEEN (no last-sha file) records the current sha SILENTLY (not an update). Missing repo (`test -e <repo>/.git`) / unset self handle (`~/.sidecar/walkie/self`) / git failure → graceful exit 0. Never reads stdin (hexa fake-stdin trap — env + files + exec only; no self-path re-spawn). NO opt-out switch (s11) — gates only on real preconditions (hexa-lang/.git present + self handle set), else silent exit 0. hexa-lang (`_walkie_hexa.hexa tick`, via `hexa run`). NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only).

plugins.name: celebrate
plugins.description: 0.1.3 (2026-06-01) hooks.json hexa-run wrapper now fails open — a compile/runtime error exits 0 (guard deny exit 2 preserved), so a transient "compile error" trailer no longer spams every command; pairs with install.hexa serial hook-precompile warming. 🎆 Stop hook that fires a fireworks celebration when the ACTIVE /domain's progress crosses to 100% (a 'novel goal' reached). On every turn-end it resolves the active domain (same convention as the `domain` skill — git repo root + `$HOME/.claude/domain-active.tsv` active pointer, else project basename; snapshot path via the repo's `DOMAINS.tape` roster) and counts `- [ ]`/`- [x]` milestones in the active DOMAIN.md (done/total; 100% = total>0 && done==total). It keeps the last-seen percent per domain in `$HOME/.sidecar/celebrate/<domain-key>.seen` and fires ONLY on the crossing (prev <100 → now =100), then records 100 so it never re-fires while it stays at 100; if it drops below 100 and re-reaches, it celebrates again (natural). On the crossing the hook (1) emits a multi-line ASCII fireworks burst + '🎆 <DOMAIN> @goal 100% 달성!' via the top-level `systemMessage` field into the chat (0.1.2 — Stop hooks do NOT support hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext), (2) rings the terminal bell (`printf '\a'`). HARD CONSTRAINT: the Claude Code TUI canvas is owned by Ink, so this hook NEVER writes ANSI/animation to /dev/tty (that would corrupt the screen) — the animation lives ONLY in the spawned separate window; the in-stream ASCII burst is static text. Non-macOS → skips (3), keeps (1)(2). UNCONDITIONAL (commons @D s11 — no env-var/flag/config escape hatch): the ONLY gates are real preconditions — an active domain exists AND has ≥1 milestone AND progress just crossed 100; no active domain / 0 milestones / not crossing → silent exit 0. hexa-lang (`_celebrate.hexa` + `_fireworks.hexa`, via `hexa run`); reads stdin via `exec("cat")` (avoids the hexa fake-FILE segfault), checks path existence via shell `test -e`, portable `$HOME`/`${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths only.

plugins.name: agentmemory
plugins.description: 0.1.0 (2026-06-02) persistent cross-session AGENT MEMORY — capture hook half of the agentmemory family (ported from rohitg00/agentmemory, Apache-2.0; AGENT CONTRACT only — NO Rust iii-engine / REST API / viewer / benchmark harness). SessionStart + PostToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) + PreCompact hook in hexa-lang (`_agentmemory_capture.hexa`, via `hexa run`; @D g1 hexa-native-first). THREE phases: (1) SessionStart injects the server-written recall snapshot for THIS project ($HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/recall/<cwd-hash>.txt) as additionalContext, so a fresh session opens with the top-N relevant memories already in context — no snapshot → silent no-op; (2) PostToolUse captures ONE salient observation (file write/edit or command) into the working-memory capture queue ($HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/queue/capture.jsonl) — a single JSON-line append, no DB, never blocks the tool call; (3) PreCompact drops a flush marker so the MCP server (agentmemory-mcp) rolls working→episodic (the upstream sleep-consolidation entry point). The 4-tier store SSOT schema (working · episodic · semantic · procedural + sessions + links) ships at store/schema.sql, applied on first use by the MCP server against $HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/mem.db (s16 runtime state OUTSIDE the repo · s3 portable paths — $HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only). ADVISORY · FAIL-OPEN — every path exits 0 on error, the deny exit-2 is never used (it nudges/queues, never blocks). NO opt-out switch (s11) — gates only on the salient-tool precondition. Pairs with agentmemory-mcp (MCP tool server) + memory (/memory command).

plugins.name: agentmemory-mcp
plugins.description: 0.3.1 (2026-06-03) FIX (first-session MCP '✘ failed') — the host now launches the STDIO server via a SYNC launch wrapper (`bin/_agentmemory_mcp_launch.sh`) that ensures `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` is vendored BEFORE `exec`ing the server, closing the race where the host's immediate session-start launch beat the up-script's DETACHED `npm install` (first session after `sidecar sync` rebuilt the cache → node_modules absent → SDK import fail → panel `failed` until next session). Warm cache → instant exec; cold → ~7s sync install then serve (within the host MCP init timeout), up-script detached pre-warm still parallel. node_modules STILL never committed (s3 · s11 · fail-open). 0.3.0 (2026-06-02) adds the deferred VECTOR-EMBEDDING stream as the 4th RRF input in `memory_smart_search` — dense cosine similarity over an OPTIONAL embedding backend (`@xenova/transformers` BGE-small, an optionalDependency). New `vectors` table (L2-normalized Float32 BLOBs), embeddings computed best-effort on `memory_save` + backfillable via the NEW `memory_reindex` tool. GRACEFUL · regression-safe (@L4): backend absent → vector stream empty → smart-search fuses the same 3 streams as 0.2.0 byte-for-byte (s11 — gate is the real precondition, no opt-out). Enable: `npm i @xenova/transformers` in server dir, then `memory_reindex`. 0.2.0 (2026-06-02) `memory_smart_search` now does REAL hybrid relevance — three independent ranking streams fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF_K=60): BM25-lite keyword (title-weighted term overlap) + recency (ts desc) + graph-link (ids linked via the `links` table to the top keyword hits). Returns `{mode:hybrid, fusion, deferred, results:[{id,tier,title,score,…}]}`; an optional `project` filter scopes the pool. The vector-embedding stream is a documented FOLLOW-UP (@L4 · deferred) — a drop-in 4th RRF stream, no signature change. 0.1.0 (2026-06-02) the MCP-tool-server half of the agentmemory family — the FIRST permitted MCP server after the s15 STDIO-MCP ban was retired (PR #382). Ported from rohitg00/agentmemory (Apache-2.0; AGENT MCP CONTRACT only — NO Rust iii-engine / REST API / viewer / benchmark harness). Mirrors the walkie-mcp precedent: a hook plugin bundling a Node MCP server (`server/agentmemory-mcp-server.mjs`) + a hexa up-script (`bin/_agentmemory_mcp_up.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Exposes SIX core memory tools over STDIO transport against the 4-tier SQLite store at `$HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/mem.db` (schema = PR1 `store/schema.sql` SSOT, bundled byte-identical at `server/schema.sql`, applied on first use): `memory_save` (save an insight/decision/fact into a chosen tier — working|episodic|semantic|procedural, default semantic), `memory_recall` (keyword recall across all 4 tiers, recency-ordered, full|compact), `memory_smart_search` (hybrid relevance — BM25-lite + recency + graph-link RRF fusion, RRF_K=60; vector stream deferred · @L4), `memory_sessions` (list recent sessions + summaries), `memory_forget` (delete a memory by id across tiers + its graph links), `memory_context` (compact top-N project snapshot + writes the SessionStart recall file the capture hook injects). On every tool call it lazily drains the PR1 capture queue (`$HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/queue/capture.jsonl`) into the `working` tier. STORE = `node:sqlite` (built into Node >= 22.5 — no native build, no extra dep). The up-script's ONLY job is an on-demand DETACHED `npm install --omit=dev @modelcontextprotocol/sdk` on first run (node_modules NEVER committed); the STDIO server itself is launched by the HOST per the `mcpServers` command config (the host owns its lifecycle — a dedicated child process, so NO stdio collide with hexa hooks). plugin.json registers a command-based `mcpServers` entry (now permitted — s15 retired). FAIL-OPEN — the up-script + server both `exit 0` on any error, never blocking SessionStart. NO opt-out switch (s11) — gates only on real preconditions (node/npm present, sdk absent). NO absolute paths ($HOME / $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only · s3). Pairs with agentmemory (capture hook) + memory (/memory command).

plugins.name: memory
plugins.description: 0.3.0 (2026-06-02) `search` reaches PARITY with the MCP server — FOUR RRF streams (BM25-lite + recency + graph-link + dense vector-embedding), closing the graph-link gap AND the deferred vector tier (@L4). Vector stream uses OPTIONAL `@xenova/transformers` (optionalDependencies in the new commands/memory/package.json); `save` embeds best-effort + NEW `reindex [--limit N]` backfills. GRACEFUL: no backend → same RRF as before; `npm i @xenova/transformers` in commands/memory/ to enable. Reads the same `vectors` table the MCP server populates. 0.2.0 (2026-06-02) adds the `import <file>` verb — fold PRIOR history into the store: a .jsonl file imports per-line objects ({content|text,title,tier,concepts,type} → its tier, or a raw capture obs {title,file,session_id} → working); any other text file (e.g. a MEMORY.md) imports each markdown bullet / `[slug](path) — desc` index line as a semantic note. Idempotent via a content-fingerprint row id (re-import skips dups). /memory — persistent cross-session AGENT MEMORY CLI over the agentmemory 4-tier SQLite store ($HOME/.sidecar/agentmemory/mem.db · shared with the agentmemory capture hook + agentmemory-mcp MCP server). The command half of the agentmemory family (ported from rohitg00/agentmemory, Apache-2.0; AGENT CONTRACT only — NO Rust iii-engine / REST API / viewer / benchmark harness). Implemented as a Node CLI (`bin/memory.mjs`) over node:sqlite (built into Node >= 22.5 — no extra dep), invoked from the slash command via the cached-bin resolve pattern. VERBS: bare/`status` (tier counts working·episodic·semantic·procedural + session count + store path) · `save <text> [--tier working|episodic|semantic|procedural] [--title T] [--concepts a,b] [--type fact|pattern|preference|architecture|bug|workflow]` (default tier semantic) · `recall <query>` (cross-tier keyword recall, recency-ordered) · `search <query>` (HYBRID RRF relevance — BM25-lite keyword + recency + graph-link Reciprocal Rank Fusion, the same algorithm as the MCP server's memory_smart_search; vector-embedding stream deferred · @L4) · `sessions` (recent sessions with project·status·obs-count) · `forget <id>` (delete a memory by id across all tiers + its graph links) · `consolidate` (the upstream SLEEP-CONSOLIDATION pipeline: working→episodic [roll a session's salient observations into one summary + drop the folded working rows] → episodic→semantic [distill a concept recurring across >=2 episodic sessions into a durable semantic pattern] → semantic→procedural [promote a multi-step semantic note to a reusable how-to]; also auto-fired on PreCompact via the capture hook's flush marker) · `import <file>` (fold prior MEMORY.md / transcript JSONL history into the store, idempotent). Verified end-to-end: the full working→episodic→semantic→procedural cascade promotes across all four tiers; import folds both .jsonl and MEMORY.md with dedup. s16 runtime state OUTSIDE the repo · s3 portable paths. Pairs with agentmemory (capture hook) + agentmemory-mcp (MCP tool server).

plugins.description: PreToolUse security reminder hook for Claude Code. Session-state caching prevents duplicate warnings; 30-day auto-cleanup. Disable per-session with ENABLE_SECURITY_REMINDER=0. Ported from David Dworken at Anthropic.

Hard rules: pricing outputs model+range (never a single number), deal outputs route to named human approver (never auto-approve), forecast outputs surface conversion assumption, RFP never invents claims for GAP requirements. 21 stdlib Python tools, 28+ reference docs. Each sub-skill ships per-skill onboarding questions (onboard.py), a customization config consumed by every tool, and an isolated opt-in autoresearch evaluator (ar_evaluator.py) bridging to engineering/autoresearch-agent. 24 stdlib Python tools (12 analysis + 12 onboarding/customization/autoresearch), 12 reference docs.
